<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/components/renderers/FootnoteRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import {
     2→  TooltipContent,
     3→  TooltipRoot,
     4→  TooltipTrigger,
     5→} from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     6→import type { HTMLAttributes, MouseEvent } from 'react'
     7→import { useCallback } from 'react'
     8→
     9→import {
    10→  useFootnoteContent,
    11→  useFootnoteDisplayNumber,
    12→} from '../../context/FootnoteDefinitionsContext'
    13→
    14→export interface FootnoteRendererProps {
    15→  identifier: string
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function FootnoteRenderer({ identifier }: FootnoteRendererProps) {
    19→  const content = useFootnoteContent(identifier)
    20→  const displayNumber = useFootnoteDisplayNumber(identifier)
    21→
    22→  const referenceId = `footnote-ref-${identifier}`
    23→  const targetId = `footnote-${identifier}`
    24→
    25→  const handleClick = useCallback(
    26→    (e: MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>) => {
    27→      const target =
    28→        document.getElementById(targetId) ||
    29→        document.getElementById(`fn-${identifier}`)
    30→      if (!target) return
    31→
    32→      e.preventDefault()
    33→      target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' })
    34→      target.classList.add('rich-footnote-highlight')
    35→
    36→      window.setTimeout(() => {
    37→        target.classList.remove('rich-footnote-highlight')
    38→      }, 1200)
    39→    },
    40→    [identifier, targetId],
    41→  )
    42→
    43→  const label = displayNumber ?? identifier
    44→
    45→  return (
    46→    <span className="rich-footnote-ref-wrapper">
    47→      <TooltipRoot>
    48→        <TooltipTrigger
    49→          render={(props: HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>) => (
    50→            <a
    51→              {...props}
    52→              className="rich-footnote-ref"
    53→              href={`#${targetId}`}
    54→              id={referenceId}
    55→              role="doc-noteref"
    56→              aria-label={
    57→                content ? `Footnote ${label}: ${content}` : `Footnote ${label}`
    58→              }
    59→              onClick={handleClick}
    60→              data-footnote-ref={identifier}
    61→            >
    62→              {label}
    63→            </a>
    64→          )}
    65→        />
    66→        {content ? <TooltipContent>{content}</TooltipContent> : null}
    67→      </TooltipRoot>
    68→    </span>
    69→  )
    70→}
    71→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 08:39:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/components/renderers/FootnoteSectionRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { MouseEvent } from 'react'
     2→import { useCallback } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import { useFootnoteDefinitions } from '../../context/FootnoteDefinitionsContext'
     5→
     6→export interface FootnoteSectionRendererProps {
     7→  definitions: Record<string, string>
     8→  nodeKey: string
     9→}
    10→
    11→export function FootnoteSectionRenderer({
    12→  definitions,
    13→}: FootnoteSectionRendererProps) {
    14→  const { displayNumberMap } = useFootnoteDefinitions()
    15→
    16→  const sortedEntries = Object.entries(definitions).sort(
    17→    ([a], [b]) => (displayNumberMap[a] ?? 0) - (displayNumberMap[b] ?? 0),
    18→  )
    19→
    20→  if (sortedEntries.length === 0) return null
    21→
    22→  return (
    23→    <div className="rich-footnote-section-content" role="doc-endnotes">
    24→      <hr className="rich-footnote-section-divider" />
    25→      <ol className="rich-footnote-section-list">
    26→        {sortedEntries.map(([identifier, content]) => {
    27→          const displayNum = displayNumberMap[identifier] ?? identifier
    28→          return (
    29→            <FootnoteSectionItem
    30→              key={identifier}
    31→              identifier={identifier}
    32→              content={content}
    33→              displayNum={displayNum}
    34→            />
    35→          )
    36→        })}
    37→      </ol>
    38→    </div>
    39→  )
    40→}
    41→
    42→function FootnoteSectionItem({
    43→  identifier,
    44→  content,
    45→  displayNum,
    46→}: {
    47→  identifier: string
    48→  content: string
    49→  displayNum: number | string
    50→}) {
    51→  const targetId = `footnote-${identifier}`
    52→  const refId = `footnote-ref-${identifier}`
    53→
    54→  const handleBackClick = useCallback(
    55→    (e: MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>) => {
    56→      e.preventDefault()
    57→      const refElement = document.getElementById(refId)
    58→      if (!refElement) return
    59→      refElement.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' })
    60→      refElement.classList.add('rich-footnote-highlight')
    61→      window.setTimeout(() => {
    62→        refElement.classList.remove('rich-footnote-highlight')
    63→      }, 1200)
    64→    },
    65→    [refId],
    66→  )
    67→
    68→  return (
    69→    <li
    70→      id={targetId}
    71→      className="rich-footnote-section-item"
    72→      value={typeof displayNum === 'number' ? displayNum : undefined}
    73→    >
    74→      <span className="rich-footnote-section-item-content">{content}</span>
    75→      <a
    76→        href={`#${refId}`}
    77→        onClick={handleBackClick}
    78→        className="rich-footnote-back-ref"
    79→        role="doc-backlink"
    80→        aria-label={`Back to reference ${displayNum}`}
    81→      >
    82→        ↩
    83→      </a>
    84→    </li>
    85→  )
    86→}
    87→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
Perfect! Here's a comprehensive summary of the rich text rendering system in the Shiroi web app:

## LexicalContent.tsx - Main Rich Text Renderer

**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx`

Core component that:
- Imports `ShiroRenderer` (aliased as `IsolateRenderer`) from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderer`
- Parses Lexical editor state JSON from a content string
- Sets up theme styling with `createThemeStyle()` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Configures LinkCardFetchProvider with adapters for external services (GitHub, TMDB, Bangumi, LeetCode, Netease Music, QQ Music, MX-Space)
- Applies theme colors (accent, link, light accent, quote borders)
- Handles typography settings with variant support (note vs default fonts)
- Injects custom CSS variables for fonts into the shadow root
- Returns null if content can't be parsed

**Key Props**:
- `content`: JSON string of SerializedEditorState
- `variant`: 'note' or default (affects font family)
- `className`: Additional CSS classes

## Usage Chain

The rendering hierarchy flows as:

1. **LexicalContent** (base component)
   ↓
2. **MainLexicalContent** - Wrapper that attaches to DOM and registers with reading atoms
   ↓
3. **Page/Post/Note Renderers** - Extract content from context providers and pass to MainLexicalContent

### Consumer Components

**Page Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/(page-detail)/[slug]/PageLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentPageDataSelector` to get page content
- Renders with default variant

**Post Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentPostDataSelector` to get post content
- Renders with default variant

**Note Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/NoteLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentNoteDataSelector` to get note content
- Renders with `variant="note"` for serif typography

**MainLexicalContent Wrapper**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/MainLexicalContent.tsx`
- Wraps LexicalContent with DOM element registration
- Sets `MAIN_MARKDOWN_ID` for reading tracking
- Calls `setMainMarkdownElement` atom to register element for intersection observer/reading progress

All found files use ShiroRenderer indirectly through LexicalContent, which bridges Lexical editor state to the Shiro-specific rendering system with theming and external embed support.

 `./apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style.css'
     4→
     5→import type {
     6→  LinkCardFetchContext,
     7→  RichEditorVariant,
     8→} from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro'
     9→import { createThemeStyle, LinkCardFetchProvider } from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro'
    10→import { IsolateRenderer as ShiroRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderer'
    11→import type { SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
    12→import { useMemo } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { useIsDark } from '~/hooks/common/use-is-dark'
    15→import { fetchGitHubApi } from '~/lib/github'
    16→
    17→// Mirror @theme inline font values into shadow root (Tailwind v4 @theme inline does not emit CSS vars)
    18→const fontVarsCss = `:host{--font-sans:var(--app-font-sans),system-ui,-apple-system,'PingFang SC','Microsoft YaHei','Segoe UI',Roboto,Helvetica,'noto sans sc','hiragino sans gb','sans-serif',Apple Color Emoji,Segoe UI Emoji,Not Color Emoji;--font-serif:'Noto Serif CJK SC','Noto Serif SC',var(--app-font-serif),'Source Han Serif SC','Source Han Serif',source-han-serif-sc,SongTi SC,SimSum,'Hiragino Sans GB',system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,Roboto,Helvetica,'Microsoft YaHei','WenQuanYi Micro Hei',sans-serif;--font-mono:'OperatorMonoSSmLig Nerd Font','Cascadia Code PL','FantasqueSansMono Nerd Font','operator mono',JetBrainsMono,'Fira code Retina','Fira code','Consolas',Monaco,'Hannotate SC',monospace,-apple-system}`
    19→
    20→const linkCardFetchContext: LinkCardFetchContext = {
    21→  adapters: {
    22→    github: {
    23→      request: fetchGitHubApi,
    24→    },
    25→    tmdb: {
    26→      request: (url) => {
    27→        const u = new URL(url)
    28→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/3\//, '')
    29→        return fetch(`/api/tmdb/${path}${u.search}`).then((r) => r.json())
    30→      },
    31→    },
    32→    bangumi: {
    33→      request: (url) => {
    34→        const u = new URL(url)
    35→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/v0\//, '')
    36→        return fetch(`/api/bangumi/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    37→      },
    38→    },
    39→    leetcode: {
    40→      request: (_url, init) => {
    41→        return fetch('/api/leetcode', init).then((r) => r.json())
    42→      },
    43→    },
    44→    'netease-music': {
    45→      request: (url) => {
    46→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    47→        return fetch('/api/music/netease', {
    48→          method: 'POST',
    49→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    50→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    51→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    52→      },
    53→    },
    54→    'qq-music': {
    55→      request: (url) => {
    56→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    57→        return fetch('/api/music/tencent', {
    58→          method: 'POST',
    59→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    60→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    61→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    62→      },
    63→    },
    64→    'mx-space': {
    65→      request: (path) => {
    66→        return fetch(`/api/v2/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    67→      },
    68→    },
    69→  },
    70→}
    71→
    72→export interface LexicalContentProps {
    73→  content: string
    74→  variant?: RichEditorVariant
    75→  className?: string
    76→}
    77→
    78→export function LexicalContent({
    79→  content,
    80→  variant,
    81→  className,
    82→}: LexicalContentProps) {
    83→  const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
    84→    try {
    85→      return JSON.parse(content)
    86→    } catch {
    87→      return null
    88→    }
    89→  }, [content])
    90→
    91→  const isDark = useIsDark()
    92→
    93→  const editorOverrideStyle = useMemo(
    94→    () =>
    95→      createThemeStyle({
    96→        color: {
    97→          accent: 'var(--color-accent)',
    98→          link: 'var(--color-accent)',
    99→          accentLight:
   100→            'color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-accent) 20%, transparent)',
   101→          quoteBorder: 'var(--color-accent)',
   102→        },
   103→        layout: { maxWidth: '100%' },
   104→        typography: {
   105→          fontFamily:
   106→            variant === 'note'
   107→              ? 'var(--note-font-override, var(--font-serif))'
   108→              : 'var(--font-sans)',
   109→          fontFamilySerif: 'var(--font-serif)',
   110→          fontMono: 'var(--font-mono)',
   111→        },
   112→      }),
   113→    [variant],
   114→  )
   115→  if (!editorState) return null
   116→
   117→  return (
   118→    <LinkCardFetchProvider value={linkCardFetchContext}>
   119→      <ShiroRenderer
   120→        value={editorState}
   121→        variant={variant}
   122→        theme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}
   123→        className={className}
   124→        style={editorOverrideStyle}
   125→        extraCss={fontVarsCss}
   126→      />
   127→    </LinkCardFetchProvider>
   128→  )
   129→}
   130→
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! Here's a comprehensive summary of the rich text rendering system in the Shiroi web app:

## LexicalContent.tsx - Main Rich Text Renderer

**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx`

Core component that:
- Imports `ShiroRenderer` (aliased as `IsolateRenderer`) from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/renderer`
- Parses Lexical editor state JSON from a content string
- Sets up theme styling with `createThemeStyle()` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Configures LinkCardFetchProvider with adapters for external services (GitHub, TMDB, Bangumi, LeetCode, Netease Music, QQ Music, MX-Space)
- Applies theme colors (accent, link, light accent, quote borders)
- Handles typography settings with variant support (note vs default fonts)
- Injects custom CSS variables for fonts into the shadow root
- Returns null if content can't be parsed

**Key Props**:
- `content`: JSON string of SerializedEditorState
- `variant`: 'note' or default (affects font family)
- `className`: Additional CSS classes

## Usage Chain

The rendering hierarchy flows as:

1. **LexicalContent** (base component)
   ↓
2. **MainLexicalContent** - Wrapper that attaches to DOM and registers with reading atoms
   ↓
3. **Page/Post/Note Renderers** - Extract content from context providers and pass to MainLexicalContent

### Consumer Components

**Page Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/(page-detail)/[slug]/PageLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentPageDataSelector` to get page content
- Renders with default variant

**Post Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentPostDataSelector` to get post content
- Renders with default variant

**Note Renderer**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/NoteLexicalRenderer.tsx`
- Uses `useCurrentNoteDataSelector` to get note content
- Renders with `variant="note"` for serif typography

**MainLexicalContent Wrapper**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/MainLexicalContent.tsx`
- Wraps LexicalContent with DOM element registration
- Sets `MAIN_MARKDOWN_ID` for reading tracking
- Calls `setMainMarkdownElement` atom to register element for intersection observer/reading progress

All found files use ShiroRenderer indirectly through LexicalContent, which bridges Lexical editor state to the Shiro-specific rendering system with theming and external embed support.

Design SSR renderer architecture

```text
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer`

### 1. Complete File Structure

```
packages/rich-ssr-renderer/
  package.json
  tsconfig.json
  vite.config.ts
  src/
    index.ts                          # Public API: RichSSRRenderer, preprocessFootnotes, SSRRendererConfig
    types.ts                          # All serialized node type definitions, SSRRendererConfig, RichSSRRendererProps
    preprocess.ts                     # Footnote pre-processing (DFS pass)
    render-node.tsx                   # Core recursive node renderer dispatch
    render-text.tsx                   # Text node rendering with format bitmask -> className
    render-element.tsx                # Built-in element nodes (paragraph, heading, quote, list, etc.)
    render-decorator.tsx              # Custom decorator nodes (image, code-block, katex, etc.)
    RichSSRRenderer.tsx               # Top-level component (entry point)
```

### 2. Core Rendering Pipeline

The pipeline is: **parse -> preprocess -> walk -> render**.

**2a. Parse**: The input `SerializedEditorState` JSON is already parsed. The top-level component receives it typed. No Lexical runtime is involved.

**2b. Preprocess**: A single synchronous pass calls `preprocessFootnotes(root)` which performs a DFS over the entire tree (including nested editor states in `alert-quote`, `banner`, `grid-container`), collecting footnote identifiers in document order, building a `displayNumberMap` and extracting `definitions` from any `footnote-section` nodes.

**2c. Walk**: The recursive `renderNode()` function dispatches on `node.type`. Each node maps to either an element renderer (for builtin element nodes) or a decorator renderer (for custom decorator nodes). The `renderNode` function passes through the `SSRRendererConfig` and `footnoteContext` as plain arguments (not React context, since RSC has no hooks).

**2d. Render**: Each specific renderer function returns JSX elements with the exact same CSS class names as the client-side Lexical theme.

### 3. Node Type -> JSX Output Mapping

#### Built-in Element Nodes (in `render-element.tsx`)

| Serialized `type` | HTML Output | CSS Class |
|---|---|---|
| `root` | Fragment wrapper, renders `children` | (none) |
| `paragraph` | `<p className="rich-paragraph">` | `rich-paragraph` |
| `heading` (tag: h1-h6) | `<h1-h6 className="rich-heading-h1" id={slugified}>` | `rich-heading-{tag}` |
| `quote` | `<blockquote className="rich-quote">` | `rich-quote` |
| `list` (listType: bullet) | `<ul className="rich-list-ul">` | `rich-list-ul` |
| `list` (listType: number) | `<ol className="rich-list-ol" start={start}>` | `rich-list-ol` |
| `list` (listType: check) | `<ul className="rich-list-ul rich-checklist">` | `rich-checklist` |
| `listitem` | `<li className="rich-list-item">` with checked variant | `rich-list-item` / `rich-list-item-checked` / `rich-list-item-unchecked` |
| `listitem` (nested) | `<li className="rich-list-nested-item">` | `rich-list-nested-item` |
| `link` / `autolink` | `<a href={url} className="rich-link" rel={rel} target={target}>` | `rich-link` |
| `code` (Lexical builtin) | `<pre className="rich-code-block"><code>` | `rich-code-block` |
| `table` | `<div className="rich-table-scrollable-wrapper"><table className="rich-table">` | `rich-table` |
| `tablerow` | `<tr>` | (none) |
| `tablecell` | `<td className="rich-table-cell">` or `<th className="rich-table-cell-header">` | `rich-table-cell` / `rich-table-cell-header` |
| `horizontalrule` | `<hr className="rich-hr" />` | `rich-hr` |
| `spoiler` | `<span className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>` (ElementNode with inline children) | `rich-spoiler` |
| `details` | `<details className="rich-details"><summary className="rich-details-summary"><span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span></summary><div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div></details>` | `rich-details` |

#### Built-in Leaf Nodes (in `render-text.tsx`)

| Serialized `type` | HTML Output |
|---|---|
| `text` | `<span className="{format classes}">{text}</span>` - multiple wrappings for combined formats |
| `linebreak` | `<br />` |
| `tab` | `\t` (tab character in text) |

#### Custom Decorator Nodes (in `render-decorator.tsx`)

Each decorator node type checks `SSRRendererConfig` for a custom renderer first, falling back to a built-in static SSR output.

| Serialized `type` | Renderer Config Key | Default SSR Output |
|---|---|---|
| `image` | `Image` | `<div className="rich-image-wrapper"><figure className="rich-image"><img src={src} alt={altText} width={width} height={height} loading="lazy" />{caption && <figcaption>{caption}</figcaption>}</figure></div>` |
| `video` | `Video` | `<div className="rich-video-wrapper"><figure className="rich-video"><video src={src} poster={poster} controls preload="metadata" /></figure></div>` |
| `code-block` | `CodeBlock` | `<div className="rich-code-block-wrapper"><div className="rich-code-block">{header}<pre><code>{code}</code></pre></div></div>` |
| `katex-inline` | `KaTeX` | `<span><code className="rich-katex-fallback">{equation}</code></span>` (actual KaTeX rendering is client-side; SSR shows raw formula) |
| `katex-block` | `KaTeX` | `<div className="rich-katex-block-wrapper"><code className="rich-katex-fallback">{equation}</code></div>` |
| `mermaid` | `Mermaid` | `<div className="rich-mermaid-wrapper"><div className="rich-mermaid-block"><pre><code>{diagram}</code></pre></div></div>` |
| `mention` | `Mention` | `<span className="rich-mention rich-mention-plain"><span className="rich-mention-handle">@{displayName or handle}</span></span>` |
| `link-card` | `LinkCard` | `<div className="rich-link-card-wrapper"><a className="rich-link-card" href={url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">...</a></div>` |
| `footnote` | `Footnote` | `<sup className="rich-footnote"><span className="rich-footnote-ref-wrapper"><a className="rich-footnote-ref" href="#footnote-{id}" id="footnote-ref-{id}" role="doc-noteref">{displayNumber}</a></span></sup>` |
| `footnote-section` | `FootnoteSection` | `<div className="rich-footnote-section"><div className="rich-footnote-section-content" role="doc-endnotes"><hr className="rich-footnote-section-divider" /><ol className="rich-footnote-section-list">...</ol></div></div>` |
| `alert-quote` | `Alert` (header only) | `<div className="rich-alert rich-alert-{type}"><div className="rich-alert-header rich-alert-header-{type}">...</div><div className="rich-alert-content">{recursively rendered content}</div></div>` |
| `banner` | `Banner` (icon only) | `<div className="rich-banner rich-banner-{type}"><div className="rich-banner-inner"><span className="rich-banner-icon rich-banner-icon-{type}" /><div className="rich-banner-content">{recursively rendered content}</div></div></div>` |
| `grid-container` | (none) | `<div className="rich-grid-container"><div className="rich-grid-inner" style={{display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat({cols}, 1fr)', gap: '{gap}'}}>{cells mapped to divs}</div></div>` |
| `spoiler` | (none) | Already handled as element node |
| `details` | (none) | Already handled as element node |
| `embed` | (none) | `<div><a href={url}>{url}</a></div>` (graceful fallback) |
| `code-snippet` | `CodeSnippet` | Render each file's code block with filename header |
| `gallery` | `Gallery` | `<div className="rich-gallery">{images.map(img => <img>)}</div>` |
| `tldraw` | (none) | `<div>[Tldraw content]</div>` (skip or placeholder) |

### 4. Footnote Pre-Processing Algorithm

```typescript
// In preprocess.ts

interface FootnoteContext {
  displayNumberMap: Record<string, number>
  definitions: Record<string, string>
}

function preprocessFootnotes(root: SerializedRootNode): FootnoteContext {
  const seen = new Set<string>()
  const numberMap: Record<string, number> = {}
  let counter = 1
  let definitions: Record<string, string> = {}

  function dfs(node: SerializedAnyNode): void {
    // Check if this is a footnote node
    if (node.type === 'footnote') {
      const id = (node as SerializedFootnoteNode).identifier
      if (!seen.has(id)) {
        seen.add(id)
        numberMap[id] = counter++
      }
    }

    // Check if this is a footnote-section node (collect definitions)
    if (node.type === 'footnote-section') {
      const defs = (node as SerializedFootnoteSectionNode).definitions
      if (defs) {
        definitions = { ...definitions, ...defs }
      }
    }

    // Recurse into children (element nodes)
    if ('children' in node && Array.isArray(node.children)) {
      for (const child of node.children) {
        dfs(child)
      }
    }

    // Recurse into nested editor states
    if (node.type === 'alert-quote' || node.type === 'banner') {
      const content = (node as any).content as SerializedEditorState | undefined
      if (content?.root?.children) {
        for (const child of content.root.children) {
          dfs(child)
        }
      }
    }

    if (node.type === 'grid-container') {
      const cells = (node as any).cells as SerializedEditorState[] | undefined
      if (cells) {
        for (const cell of cells) {
          if (cell?.root?.children) {
            for (const child of cell.root.children) {
              dfs(child)
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  // Walk all top-level children
  for (const child of root.children) {
    dfs(child)
  }

  return { displayNumberMap: numberMap, definitions }
}
```

Key design decisions:
- Single DFS pass before any rendering begins
- Traverses into nested `SerializedEditorState` (alert-quote, banner, grid-container)
- Assigns sequential display numbers, deduplicating by identifier (same logic as `FootnotePlugin`)
- Collects definitions from `footnote-section` nodes

### 5. Renderer Config Injection Mechanism

Since React Server Components cannot use hooks or context, the config is passed as **props** through the rendering tree.

```typescript
// In types.ts

export interface SSRRendererConfig {
  Alert?: ComponentType<AlertRendererProps>
  Banner?: ComponentType<BannerRendererProps>
  CodeBlock?: ComponentType<CodeBlockRendererProps>
  CodeSnippet?: ComponentType<CodeSnippetRendererProps>
  Footnote?: ComponentType<SSRFootnoteRendererProps>    // SSR variant (no tooltip)
  FootnoteSection?: ComponentType<SSRFootnoteSectionRendererProps>
  Gallery?: ComponentType<GalleryRendererProps>
  Image?: ComponentType<ImageRendererProps>
  KaTeX?: ComponentType<KaTeXRendererProps>
  LinkCard?: ComponentType<LinkCardRendererProps>
  Mermaid?: ComponentType<MermaidRendererProps>
  Mention?: ComponentType<MentionRendererProps>
  Video?: ComponentType<VideoRendererProps>
}

// Internal rendering context (plain object, not React context)
interface RenderContext {
  rendererConfig?: SSRRendererConfig
  footnoteContext: FootnoteContext
  unknownNodeStrategy: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug'
}
```

The `RenderContext` object is threaded through every `renderNode()` call as a plain function argument:

```typescript
function renderNode(node: SerializedAnyNode, ctx: RenderContext, key: string | number): ReactNode
```

For the top-level component:

```typescript
interface RichSSRRendererProps {
  value: SerializedEditorState
  variant?: RichEditorVariant      // 'article' | 'note' | 'comment'
  theme?: 'light' | 'dark'
  className?: string
  style?: CSSProperties
  as?: ElementType
  rendererConfig?: SSRRendererConfig
  unknownNodeStrategy?: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug'
}
```

### 6. Nested Editor State Handling

Three node types contain nested `SerializedEditorState`: `alert-quote` (field: `content`), `banner` (field: `content`), `grid-container` (field: `cells: SerializedEditorState[]`).

The approach is to call a shared helper `renderEditorState()` that takes a `SerializedEditorState` and the `RenderContext`, renders `state.root.children` by mapping each child through `renderNode()`:

```typescript
// In render-node.tsx

function renderEditorState(
  state: SerializedEditorState,
  ctx: RenderContext,
): ReactNode {
  if (!state?.root?.children) return null
  return state.root.children.map((child, i) => renderNode(child, ctx, i))
}
```

For alert-quote:
```tsx
<div className={`rich-alert rich-alert-${alertType}`}>
  {AlertComponent ? <AlertComponent type={alertType} /> : <DefaultAlertHeader type={alertType} />}
  <div className="rich-alert-content">
    {renderEditorState(node.content, ctx)}
  </div>
</div>
```

For banner:
```tsx
<div className={`rich-banner rich-banner-${bannerType}`}>
  <div className="rich-banner-inner">
    {BannerComponent ? <BannerComponent type={bannerType} /> : <DefaultBannerIcon type={bannerType} />}
    <div className="rich-banner-content">
      {renderEditorState(node.content, ctx)}
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

For grid-container:
```tsx
<div className="rich-grid-container">
  <div className="rich-grid-inner" style={{ display: 'grid', gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, 1fr)`, gap }}>
    {cells.map((cellState, i) => (
      <div key={i} className="rich-grid-cell">
        {renderEditorState(cellState, ctx)}
      </div>
    ))}
  </div>
</div>
```

The key insight: the same `RenderContext` (including `footnoteContext` and `rendererConfig`) is passed into nested state rendering, so footnotes inside nested states are correctly numbered using the pre-computed `displayNumberMap`.

### 7. Text Format Bitmask -> className Mapping

From the Lexical source, the text format bitmask values are:

```typescript
// In render-text.tsx

const FORMAT_BOLD = 1
const FORMAT_ITALIC = 2
const FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH = 4
const FORMAT_UNDERLINE = 8
const FORMAT_CODE = 16
const FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT = 32
const FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT = 64
const FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT = 128

const FORMAT_CLASS_MAP: [number, string][] = [
  [FORMAT_BOLD, 'rich-text-bold'],
  [FORMAT_ITALIC, 'rich-text-italic'],
  [FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH, 'rich-text-strikethrough'],
  [FORMAT_UNDERLINE, 'rich-text-underline'],
  [FORMAT_CODE, 'rich-text-code'],
  [FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT, 'rich-text-subscript'],
  [FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT, 'rich-text-superscript'],
  [FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT, 'rich-text-highlight'],
]

function getTextFormatClasses(format: number): string {
  const classes: string[] = []
  for (const [mask, cls] of FORMAT_CLASS_MAP) {
    if (format & mask) classes.push(cls)
  }
  return classes.join(' ')
}
```

The text node renderer:

```tsx
function renderTextNode(node: SerializedTextNode, key: string | number): ReactNode {
  if (node.type === 'linebreak') return <br key={key} />
  if (node.type === 'tab') return '\t'

  const text = node.text
  const format = node.format || 0

  if (format === 0) return text

  const className = getTextFormatClasses(format)
  return <span key={key} className={className}>{text}</span>
}
```

This matches the Lexical theme exactly because the `editorTheme` in `theme.ts` defines:
- `text.bold` -> `rich-text-bold`
- `text.italic` -> `rich-text-italic`
- etc.

When multiple formats are combined (e.g., bold + italic), Lexical applies all class names to a single `<span>`. We replicate this by joining all matching class names.

### 8. Error Handling for Malformed Input

Three strategies, controlled by the `unknownNodeStrategy` prop (default: `'skip'`):

```typescript
function renderUnknownNode(
  node: SerializedAnyNode,
  strategy: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug',
  key: string | number,
): ReactNode {
  switch (strategy) {
    case 'skip':
      return null

    case 'text':
      // Attempt to extract text content from children
      if ('children' in node && Array.isArray(node.children)) {
        return walkText(node)
      }
      if ('text' in node && typeof node.text === 'string') {
        return node.text
      }
      return null

    case 'debug':
      return (
        <div
          key={key}
          style={{
            border: '1px dashed red',
            padding: '4px',
            fontSize: '12px',
            fontFamily: 'monospace',
          }}
        >
          Unknown node: {node.type}
        </div>
      )
  }
}
```

Additional safeguards:
- The top-level `RichSSRRenderer` wraps rendering in a try-catch; on failure, returns `null` and logs a warning
- Individual node renderers gracefully handle missing/malformed properties with defaults
- `preprocessFootnotes` is fault-tolerant: missing `children` arrays are treated as empty

### 9. Integration Point with the Web App

The current rendering chain in the web app is:

```
LexicalContent.tsx ('use client')
  -> IsolateRenderer (Shadow DOM wrapper)
    -> ShiroRenderer
      -> RichRenderer (uses LexicalComposer)
```

The SSR integration adds a new path that **replaces this entire chain for server rendering**, while keeping the client renderer for hydration / interactive features.

**Option A: Direct replacement for read-only pages (recommended)**

For pages that only display content (post detail, note detail, pages), the web app can use the SSR renderer directly in Server Components without any `'use client'` directive:

```tsx
// In a Server Component (e.g., apps/web/src/app/(main)/posts/[slug]/page.tsx)
import { RichSSRRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer'
import { ssrRendererConfig } from './ssr-config' // app-level SSR renderers

// This renders real HTML on the server - no empty contentEditable shells
<RichSSRRenderer
  value={editorState}
  variant="article"
  className="rich-content"
  rendererConfig={ssrRendererConfig}
/>
```

**Option B: Hybrid approach (SSR + client hydration)**

For pages that need interactive features (spoiler click, footnote tooltip), render SSR HTML for initial paint, then hydrate with the client renderer:

```tsx
// Server component renders visible HTML
<Suspense fallback={<RichSSRRenderer value={editorState} variant="article" />}>
  <LexicalContent content={serialized} variant="article" />
</Suspense>
```

**Integration into `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`**

Add a new export path to `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`:

```
"./ssr": "./src/ssr.tsx"
```

This `ssr.tsx` would create a `ShiroSSRRenderer` that wraps `RichSSRRenderer` with the same `enhancedRendererConfig` adapted for SSR (using the SSR-compatible subset of renderers).

### 10. Phased Implementation Order

**Phase 1: Foundation (files: `types.ts`, `render-text.tsx`, `render-element.tsx`)**
- Define all serialized node type interfaces in `types.ts`
- Implement text format bitmask rendering
- Implement all built-in element nodes (paragraph, heading, quote, list, link, table, hr, code, spoiler, details)
- Result: Can render any document that uses only built-in Lexical nodes

**Phase 2: Core decorator nodes (`render-decorator.tsx`, `preprocess.ts`)**
- Implement simple decorator nodes: image, video, code-block, mention, link-card, mermaid, katex (inline/block)
- Implement footnote pre-processing
- Implement footnote and footnote-section rendering
- Result: Can render most real-world documents

**Phase 3: Nested editor states (additions to `render-decorator.tsx`)**
- Implement alert-quote with recursive `renderEditorState`
- Implement banner with recursive `renderEditorState`
- Implement grid-container with recursive `renderEditorState` over cells array
- Result: Full nested content support

**Phase 4: Renderer config + top-level component (`RichSSRRenderer.tsx`, `index.ts`)**
- Wire up `SSRRendererConfig` prop threading
- Implement `RichSSRRenderer` top-level component with variant class, error handling
- Implement unknown node strategy
- Set up package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json
- Result: Complete standalone package

**Phase 5: Extension nodes**
- Implement embed, code-snippet, gallery, tldraw (placeholder) rendering
- Handle legacy serialized formats (banner with `children` instead of `content`)

**Phase 6: Integration (`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro` changes)**
- Add `./ssr` export to `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Create `ShiroSSRRenderer` that wires up `enhancedRendererConfig` (SSR-compatible subset)
- Update `LexicalContent.tsx` in web app to use SSR renderer for initial render

**Phase 7: Testing + Polish**
- Verify CSS class name parity with client renderer
- Test all node types with real serialized data from the blog
- Verify footnote numbering matches client-side behavior
- Test edge cases: empty documents, deeply nested structures, malformed JSON

---

### Package Configuration Details

**package.json** (following `@haklex/rich-headless` pattern):

```json
{
  "name": "@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "Server-side renderer for Lexical serialized state (zero Lexical runtime)",
  "license": "MIT",
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    ".": "./src/index.ts"
  },
  "main": "./src/index.ts",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev:build": "vite build --watch"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/react": "^19.2.14",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "vite": "^7.3.1",
    "vite-plugin-dts": "^4.5.4"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "react": ">=19"
  },
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public",
    "exports": {
      ".": {
        "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts"
      }
    },
    "main": "./dist/index.mjs",
    "types": "./dist/index.d.ts"
  }
}
```

Key: zero `dependencies` section. Only `react` as peer dep. No Lexical, no browser APIs.

**vite.config.ts**:

```typescript
import { createViteConfig } from '../vite.shared'

export default createViteConfig({
  vanillaExtract: false,
  esbuild: { jsx: 'automatic', jsxImportSource: 'react' },
})
```

**tsconfig.json** (following rich-headless, but adding JSX support):

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2021",
    "lib": ["ES2021"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "outDir": "dist",
    "jsx": "react-jsx"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Note: `lib` does NOT include `"DOM"` -- this enforces the zero-browser-API constraint at compile time. Any accidental `document` or `window` reference will be a type error.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/styles/theme.ts` - Source of truth for all CSS class names that the SSR renderer must replicate exactly
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/types/renderer-config.ts` - Defines `RendererConfig` interface and all renderer prop types; the SSR equivalent (`SSRRendererConfig`) must mirror these keys
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx` - Contains the canonical footnote numbering algorithm (DFS over `FootnoteNode` instances, deduplication, sequential counter) that must be replicated for serialized JSON
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts` - Defines all node type strings and their property shapes (the headless decorator definitions serve as the canonical reference for which properties each serialized node type carries)
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/ShiroRenderer.tsx` - Integration point pattern; the SSR equivalent (`ShiroSSRRenderer`) will follow this same structure of composing `enhancedRendererConfig` with the base renderer

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-02-25 08:41:27Z)**_

Design the implementation plan for a new package `@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer` in the Shiroi monorepo. Here's the comprehensive context:

## Current Problem
`RichRenderer` (in `@haklex/rich-editor`) uses `LexicalComposer` which requires browser DOM. During SSR, it outputs empty `contentEditable` div shells with no actual content, harming SEO and first-paint.

## Goal  
A pure React component that takes `SerializedEditorState` JSON and outputs semantic HTML without any Lexical runtime or browser API dependency. Must work in Next.js Server Components.

## Existing Architecture
- **Monorepo**: pnpm + Turborepo, packages in `/packages/*`
- **Build**: Shared Vite config at `packages/vite.shared.ts` — ESM, `vite-plugin-dts`, optional Vanilla Extract
- **@haklex/rich-headless**: Already has all node type definitions as headless Lexical nodes (no React). Has `extractText()` and `walkChildren()` helpers for text extraction from serialized state.
- **@haklex/rich-editor**: Has CSS class names in `theme.ts` (e.g. `rich-text-bold`, `rich-heading-h1`, `rich-paragraph`, etc.), renderer config types in `types/renderer-config.ts`
- **@haklex/rich-kit-shiro**: Integration bundle, `ShiroRenderer` wraps `RichRenderer` with enhanced renderer config

## Serialized Data Format
```typescript
SerializedEditorState = {
  root: {
    children: SerializedLexicalNode[]  // recursive tree
    direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    format: string  // element alignment
    indent: number
    type: 'root'
    version: 1
  }
}
```

Node types in the tree:
- **Builtin element nodes**: paragraph, heading (tag: h1-h6), quote, list (listType: bullet/number/check), listitem (value, checked), link (url, rel, target), autolink, code (Lexical code block), table, tablerow, tablecell
- **Builtin leaf nodes**: text (format bitmask, text string, style, mode, detail), linebreak, tab
- **Custom decorator nodes**: spoiler, mention, footnote, footnote-section, katex-inline, katex-block, image, video, link-card, code-block, mermaid, details, alert-quote, banner, grid-container, embed, code-snippet, gallery, tldraw
- **Nested editor states**: alert-quote has `content: SerializedEditorState`, banner has `content: SerializedEditorState`, grid-container has `cells: SerializedEditorState[]`

## Text Format Bitmask (Lexical)
```
IS_BOLD = 1, IS_ITALIC = 2, IS_STRIKETHROUGH = 4, IS_UNDERLINE = 8,
IS_CODE = 16, IS_SUBSCRIPT = 32, IS_SUPERSCRIPT = 64, IS_HIGHLIGHT = 128
```

## CSS Class Names (from theme.ts, must match exactly)
```
text: bold/italic/underline/strikethrough/superscript/subscript/code/highlight → rich-text-{name}
heading: h1-h6 → rich-heading-{h1-h6}
list: ol/ul/listitem/checklist/nested → rich-list-{ol/ul}/rich-list-item/rich-checklist
quote → rich-quote, link → rich-link, paragraph → rich-paragraph
table → rich-table/rich-table-cell/rich-table-cell-header
hr → rich-hr, code → rich-code-block

Custom: rich-spoiler, rich-mention, rich-footnote-ref-wrapper, rich-footnote-ref,
rich-katex-block-wrapper, rich-image-wrapper, rich-video-wrapper,
rich-code-block-wrapper, rich-alert/rich-alert-{type},
rich-banner/rich-banner-{type}, rich-details, rich-grid-container,
rich-link-card-wrapper, rich-mermaid-wrapper, rich-footnote-section,
rich-footnote-section-divider, rich-footnote-section-list, etc.
```

## Footnote System (critical to replicate)
FootnotePlugin in client-side:
1. DFS collects all `FootnoteNode` by order of appearance
2. Assigns sequential display numbers (deduplicating by identifier)
3. Auto-creates FootnoteSectionNode with definitions
4. Provides `displayNumberMap` + `definitions` via React context

SSR must replicate this: pre-process pass collects footnote order, then renders inline refs with correct numbers and the section at end.

## RendererConfig (for custom renderer injection)
```typescript
interface RendererConfig {
  Alert?, Banner?, CodeBlock?, CodeSnippet?, Footnote?, FootnoteSection?,
  Gallery?, Image?, KaTeX?, LinkCard?, Mermaid?, Mention?, Video?
}
```

## Design Requirements
1. **Zero Lexical dependency** — only reads the JSON structure, does NOT instantiate Lexical editor
2. **Zero browser API** — no window/document/navigator
3. **React Server Component compatible** — pure function, no hooks/effects/state
4. **Same CSS classes** — outputs HTML using exact same class names so existing stylesheets work
5. **Configurable renderers** — SSRRendererConfig with same keys as RendererConfig
6. **Recursive rendering** — handles nested editor states in alert-quote, banner, grid-container
7. **Footnote pre-processing** — single DFS pass to build displayNumberMap before rendering
8. **Graceful degradation** — unknown nodes handled via strategy (skip/text/debug)
9. **Type-safe** — full TypeScript with proper serialized node type definitions

## Constraints
- Package lives at `/packages/rich-ssr-renderer/`
- Use the shared Vite build config from `packages/vite.shared.ts`
- Follow the same package.json pattern as `@haklex/rich-headless`
- Dependencies: only `react` as peer dep. No Lexical, no browser APIs
- Reuse CSS from `@haklex/rich-editor/style.css` or `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style.css` (no new CSS)

Please design:
1. The complete file structure
2. Core rendering pipeline (parse → preprocess → walk → render)
3. How each node type maps to JSX output
4. The footnote pre-processing algorithm
5. The renderer config injection mechanism (props-based, not context-based since RSC)
6. How nested editor states are handled recursively
7. The text format bitmask → className mapping
8. Error handling for malformed input
9. Integration point with the web app (how to replace current ShiroRenderer for SSR)
10. Phased implementation order

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/vite.shared.ts`

```text
     1→import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import type { UserConfig } from 'vite'
     5→
     6→export interface SharedViteOptions {
     7→  entry?: string | Record<string, string>
     8→  vanillaExtract?: boolean
     9→  esbuild?: { jsx: string; jsxImportSource: string }
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BASE_EXTERNALS = ['react', 'react-dom', 'react/jsx-runtime']
    13→
    14→export async function createViteConfig(
    15→  options: SharedViteOptions = {},
    16→): Promise<UserConfig> {
    17→  const { entry = 'src/index.ts', vanillaExtract = true, esbuild } = options
    18→
    19→  const dts = (await import('vite-plugin-dts')).default
    20→  const plugins: UserConfig['plugins'] = []
    21→
    22→  if (vanillaExtract) {
    23→    const { vanillaExtractPlugin } =
    24→      await import('@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin')
    25→    plugins.push(vanillaExtractPlugin())
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  plugins.push(
    29→    dts({ entryRoot: 'src', outDir: 'dist', tsconfigPath: './tsconfig.json' }),
    30→  )
    31→
    32→  const cwd = process.cwd()
    33→  const isMap = typeof entry !== 'string'
    34→  const libEntry = isMap
    35→    ? Object.fromEntries(
    36→        Object.entries(entry).map(([k, v]) => [k, resolve(cwd, v)]),
    37→      )
    38→    : resolve(cwd, entry)
    39→
    40→  const fileName = isMap
    41→    ? (_: string, name: string) => `${name}.mjs`
    42→    : () => 'index.mjs'
    43→
    44→  const pkg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(resolve(cwd, 'package.json'), 'utf-8'))
    45→  const depNames = new Set([
    46→    ...BASE_EXTERNALS,
    47→    ...Object.keys(pkg.dependencies || {}),
    48→    ...Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies || {}),
    49→  ])
    50→
    51→  const rollupExternal = (id: string) => {
    52→    if (id[0] === '.' || id[0] === '/' || id[0] === '\0') return false
    53→    if (depNames.has(id)) return true
    54→    const slashIdx = id.indexOf('/')
    55→    if (slashIdx > 0) {
    56→      const pkgName =
    57→        id[0] === '@'
    58→          ? id.slice(0, id.indexOf('/', slashIdx + 1))
    59→          : id.slice(0, slashIdx)
    60→      return pkgName ? depNames.has(pkgName) : false
    61→    }
    62→    return false
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  return {
    66→    plugins,
    67→    ...(esbuild && { esbuild }),
    68→    build: {
    69→      lib: { entry: libEntry, formats: ['es'], fileName },
    70→      rollupOptions: {
    71→        external: rollupExternal,
    72→        output: { preserveModules: false },
    73→      },
    74→      cssCodeSplit: false,
    75→      minify: false,
    76→      cssMinify: true,
    77→      target: 'es2020',
    78→    },
    79→  }
    80→}
    81→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * @haklex/rich-headless
     3→ *
     4→ * Headless-compatible Lexical node registry for server-side parsing.
     5→ * Zero React dependency — only lexical + standard @lexical/* packages.
     6→ *
     7→ * Usage with @lexical/headless:
     8→ *   import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
     9→ *   import { allHeadlessNodes } from '@haklex/rich-headless'
    10→ *   const editor = createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allHeadlessNodes })
    11→ */
    12→
    13→import { CodeHighlightNode, CodeNode } from '@lexical/code'
    14→import { HorizontalRuleNode } from '@lexical/extension'
    15→import { AutoLinkNode, LinkNode } from '@lexical/link'
    16→import { ListItemNode, ListNode } from '@lexical/list'
    17→import { HeadingNode, QuoteNode } from '@lexical/rich-text'
    18→import { TableCellNode, TableNode, TableRowNode } from '@lexical/table'
    19→import {
    20→  DecoratorNode,
    21→  type EditorConfig,
    22→  ElementNode,
    23→  type Klass,
    24→  type LexicalEditor,
    25→  type LexicalNode,
    26→  type NodeKey,
    27→  type SerializedElementNode,
    28→  type SerializedLexicalNode,
    29→} from 'lexical'
    30→
    31→// ── Builtin nodes ──
    32→
    33→export const builtinNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
    34→  HeadingNode,
    35→  QuoteNode,
    36→  ListNode,
    37→  ListItemNode,
    38→  LinkNode,
    39→  AutoLinkNode,
    40→  HorizontalRuleNode,
    41→  TableNode,
    42→  TableCellNode,
    43→  TableRowNode,
    44→  CodeNode,
    45→  CodeHighlightNode,
    46→]
    47→
    48→// ── Helpers ──
    49→
    50→function stubDOM(): HTMLElement {
    51→  return document.createElement('span')
    52→}
    53→
    54→function extractText(state: unknown): string {
    55→  if (!state || typeof state !== 'object') return ''
    56→  const root = (state as Record<string, unknown>).root as
    57→    | Record<string, unknown>
    58→    | undefined
    59→  if (!root) return ''
    60→  return walkChildren(root)
    61→}
    62→
    63→function walkChildren(node: Record<string, unknown>): string {
    64→  const children = node.children as Record<string, unknown>[] | undefined
    65→  if (!children) return (node.text as string) ?? ''
    66→  return children.map(walkChildren).join('\n')
    67→}
    68→
    69→// ── SpoilerNode (ElementNode, inline) ──
    70→
    71→export class SpoilerNode extends ElementNode {
    72→  static getType(): string {
    73→    return 'spoiler'
    74→  }
    75→  static clone(node: SpoilerNode): SpoilerNode {
    76→    return new SpoilerNode((node as any).__key)
    77→  }
    78→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
    79→    super(key)
    80→  }
    81→  static importJSON(_json: SerializedElementNode): SpoilerNode {
    82→    return new SpoilerNode()
    83→  }
    84→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode {
    85→    return { ...super.exportJSON(), type: 'spoiler', version: 1 }
    86→  }
    87→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
    88→    return stubDOM()
    89→  }
    90→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    91→    return false
    92→  }
    93→  isInline(): boolean {
    94→    return true
    95→  }
    96→}
    97→
    98→// ── DetailsNode (ElementNode, block) ──
    99→
   100→export class DetailsNode extends ElementNode {
   101→  __summary = ''
   102→  __open = false
   103→
   104→  static getType(): string {
   105→    return 'details'
   106→  }
   107→  static clone(node: DetailsNode): DetailsNode {
   108→    const n = new DetailsNode((node as any).__key)
   109→    n.__summary = node.__summary
   110→    n.__open = node.__open
   111→    return n
   112→  }
   113→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   114→    super(key)
   115→  }
   116→  static importJSON(
   117→    json: SerializedElementNode & { summary?: string; open?: boolean },
   118→  ): DetailsNode {
   119→    const node = new DetailsNode()
   120→    node.__summary = json.summary ?? ''
   121→    node.__open = json.open ?? false
   122→    return node
   123→  }
   124→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode & { summary: string; open: boolean } {
   125→    return {
   126→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   127→      type: 'details',
   128→      summary: this.__summary,
   129→      open: this.__open,
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   133→    return stubDOM()
   134→  }
   135→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   136→    return false
   137→  }
   138→}
   139→
   140→// ── Generic headless DecoratorNode ──
   141→
   142→type Props = Record<string, unknown>
   143→
   144→function headlessDecorator(
   145→  type: string,
   146→  propKeys: string[],
   147→  defaults: Props,
   148→  inline = false,
   149→) {
   150→  class Node extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   151→    constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   152→      super(key)
   153→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   154→        ;(this as any)[`__${k}`] = defaults[k]
   155→      }
   156→    }
   157→    static getType(): string {
   158→      return type
   159→    }
   160→    static clone(node: Node): Node {
   161→      const n = new Node((node as any).__key)
   162→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   163→        ;(n as any)[`__${k}`] = (node as any)[`__${k}`]
   164→      }
   165→      return n
   166→    }
   167→    static importJSON(json: SerializedLexicalNode & Props): Node {
   168→      const node = new Node()
   169→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   170→        ;(node as any)[`__${k}`] = json[k] ?? defaults[k]
   171→      }
   172→      return node
   173→    }
   174→    exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode & Props {
   175→      const out: Props = { type, version: 1 }
   176→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   177→        out[k] = (this as any)[`__${k}`]
   178→      }
   179→      return out as SerializedLexicalNode & Props
   180→    }
   181→    createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   182→      return stubDOM()
   183→    }
   184→    updateDOM(): boolean {
   185→      return false
   186→    }
   187→    decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): null {
   188→      return null
   189→    }
   190→    isInline(): boolean {
   191→      return inline
   192→    }
   193→  }
   194→  return Node as unknown as Klass<LexicalNode>
   195→}
   196→
   197→// ── Simple property-based nodes ──
   198→
   199→export const ImageNode = headlessDecorator(
   200→  'image',
   201→  ['src', 'altText', 'width', 'height', 'caption', 'thumbhash', 'accent'],
   202→  {
   203→    src: '',
   204→    altText: '',
   205→    width: undefined,
   206→    height: undefined,
   207→    caption: undefined,
   208→    thumbhash: undefined,
   209→    accent: undefined,
   210→  },
   211→)
   212→
   213→export const VideoNode = headlessDecorator(
   214→  'video',
   215→  ['src', 'poster', 'width', 'height'],
   216→  { src: '', poster: undefined, width: undefined, height: undefined },
   217→)
   218→
   219→export const LinkCardNode = headlessDecorator(
   220→  'link-card',
   221→  ['url', 'source', 'id', 'title', 'description', 'favicon', 'image'],
   222→  {
   223→    url: '',
   224→    source: undefined,
   225→    id: undefined,
   226→    title: undefined,
   227→    description: undefined,
   228→    favicon: undefined,
   229→    image: undefined,
   230→  },
   231→)
   232→
   233→export const KaTeXInlineNode = headlessDecorator(
   234→  'katex-inline',
   235→  ['equation'],
   236→  { equation: '' },
   237→  true,
   238→)
   239→
   240→export const KaTeXBlockNode = headlessDecorator('katex-block', ['equation'], {
   241→  equation: '',
   242→})
   243→
   244→export const MermaidNode = headlessDecorator('mermaid', ['diagram'], {
   245→  diagram: '',
   246→})
   247→
   248→export const MentionNode = headlessDecorator(
   249→  'mention',
   250→  ['platform', 'handle', 'displayName'],
   251→  { platform: '', handle: '', displayName: undefined },
   252→  true,
   253→)
   254→
   255→export const CodeBlockNode = headlessDecorator(
   256→  'code-block',
   257→  ['code', 'language'],
   258→  { code: '', language: '' },
   259→)
   260→
   261→export const FootnoteNode = headlessDecorator(
   262→  'footnote',
   263→  ['identifier'],
   264→  { identifier: '' },
   265→  true,
   266→)
   267→
   268→export const FootnoteSectionNode = headlessDecorator(
   269→  'footnote-section',
   270→  ['definitions'],
   271→  { definitions: {} },
   272→)
   273→
   274→export const EmbedNode = headlessDecorator('embed', ['url', 'source'], {
   275→  url: '',
   276→  source: null,
   277→})
   278→
   279→export const CodeSnippetNode = headlessDecorator('code-snippet', ['files'], {
   280→  files: [],
   281→})
   282→
   283→export const GalleryNode = headlessDecorator('gallery', ['images', 'layout'], {
   284→  images: [],
   285→  layout: 'grid',
   286→})
   287→
   288→export const TldrawNode = headlessDecorator('tldraw', ['snapshot'], {
   289→  snapshot: '',
   290→})
   291→
   292→// ── Nested content nodes ──
   293→
   294→export class BannerNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   295→  __bannerType = 'note'
   296→  __contentState: unknown = null
   297→
   298→  static getType(): string {
   299→    return 'banner'
   300→  }
   301→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   302→    super(key)
   303→  }
   304→  static clone(node: BannerNode): BannerNode {
   305→    const n = new BannerNode((node as any).__key)
   306→    n.__bannerType = node.__bannerType
   307→    n.__contentState = node.__contentState
   308→    return n
   309→  }
   310→  static importJSON(
   311→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   312→      bannerType?: string
   313→      content?: unknown
   314→    },
   315→  ): BannerNode {
   316→    const node = new BannerNode()
   317→    node.__bannerType = json.bannerType ?? 'note'
   318→    node.__contentState = json.content ?? null
   319→    return node
   320→  }
   321→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   322→    return {
   323→      type: 'banner',
   324→      version: 1,
   325→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   326→      content: this.__contentState,
   327→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   328→  }
   329→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   330→    return stubDOM()
   331→  }
   332→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   333→    return false
   334→  }
   335→  decorate(): null {
   336→    return null
   337→  }
   338→  getTextContent(): string {
   339→    return extractText(this.__contentState)
   340→  }
   341→}
   342→
   343→export class AlertQuoteNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   344→  __alertType = 'note'
   345→  __contentState: unknown = null
   346→
   347→  static getType(): string {
   348→    return 'alert-quote'
   349→  }
   350→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   351→    super(key)
   352→  }
   353→  static clone(node: AlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
   354→    const n = new AlertQuoteNode((node as any).__key)
   355→    n.__alertType = node.__alertType
   356→    n.__contentState = node.__contentState
   357→    return n
   358→  }
   359→  static importJSON(
   360→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   361→      alertType?: string
   362→      content?: unknown
   363→    },
   364→  ): AlertQuoteNode {
   365→    const node = new AlertQuoteNode()
   366→    node.__alertType = json.alertType ?? 'note'
   367→    node.__contentState = json.content ?? null
   368→    return node
   369→  }
   370→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   371→    return {
   372→      type: 'alert-quote',
   373→      version: 1,
   374→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   375→      content: this.__contentState,
   376→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   377→  }
   378→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   379→    return stubDOM()
   380→  }
   381→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   382→    return false
   383→  }
   384→  decorate(): null {
   385→    return null
   386→  }
   387→  getTextContent(): string {
   388→    return extractText(this.__contentState)
   389→  }
   390→}
   391→
   392→export class GridContainerNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   393→  __cols = 2
   394→  __gap = '16px'
   395→  __cells: unknown[] = []
   396→
   397→  static getType(): string {
   398→    return 'grid-container'
   399→  }
   400→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   401→    super(key)
   402→  }
   403→  static clone(node: GridContainerNode): GridContainerNode {
   404→    const n = new GridContainerNode((node as any).__key)
   405→    n.__cols = node.__cols
   406→    n.__gap = node.__gap
   407→    n.__cells = node.__cells
   408→    return n
   409→  }
   410→  static importJSON(
   411→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   412→      cols?: number
   413→      gap?: string | number
   414→      cells?: unknown[]
   415→    },
   416→  ): GridContainerNode {
   417→    const node = new GridContainerNode()
   418→    node.__cols = json.cols ?? 2
   419→    const rawGap = json.gap
   420→    node.__gap = typeof rawGap === 'number' ? `${rawGap}px` : (rawGap ?? '16px')
   421→    node.__cells = json.cells ?? []
   422→    return node
   423→  }
   424→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   425→    return {
   426→      type: 'grid-container',
   427→      version: 1,
   428→      cols: this.__cols,
   429→      gap: this.__gap,
   430→      cells: this.__cells,
   431→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   432→  }
   433→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   434→    return stubDOM()
   435→  }
   436→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   437→    return false
   438→  }
   439→  decorate(): null {
   440→    return null
   441→  }
   442→  getTextContent(): string {
   443→    return this.__cells.map((cell) => extractText(cell)).join('\n')
   444→  }
   445→}
   446→
   447→// ── Aggregate ──
   448→
   449→export const customHeadlessNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
   450→  SpoilerNode,
   451→  DetailsNode,
   452→  ImageNode,
   453→  VideoNode,
   454→  LinkCardNode,
   455→  KaTeXInlineNode,
   456→  KaTeXBlockNode,
   457→  MermaidNode,
   458→  MentionNode,
   459→  CodeBlockNode,
   460→  FootnoteNode,
   461→  FootnoteSectionNode,
   462→  EmbedNode,
   463→  CodeSnippetNode,
   464→  GalleryNode,
   465→  TldrawNode,
   466→  BannerNode,
   467→  AlertQuoteNode,
   468→  GridContainerNode,
   469→]
   470→
   471→export const allHeadlessNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
   472→  ...builtinNodes,
   473→  ...customHeadlessNodes,
   474→]
   475→
   476→// ── Transformers (Lexical → Markdown) ──
   477→
   478→export { $toMarkdown,allHeadlessTransformers } from './transformers'
   479→
```

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Headless Markdown transformers — export-only (Lexical → Markdown).
     3→ * Import stubs are no-ops; used only for $convertToMarkdownString.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import {
     7→  $convertToMarkdownString,
     8→  CHECK_LIST,
     9→  type ElementTransformer,
    10→  type TextFormatTransformer,
    11→  type TextMatchTransformer,
    12→  TRANSFORMERS,
    13→} from '@lexical/markdown'
    14→import { $isTableCellNode, $isTableNode, $isTableRowNode } from '@lexical/table'
    15→
    16→// ── Helpers ──
    17→
    18→const NEVER = /a^/
    19→const NOOP = () => {}
    20→
    21→function qA(v: string): string {
    22→  return v.replaceAll('"', '\\"')
    23→}
    24→
    25→function escCell(v: string): string {
    26→  return v.replaceAll('|', '\\|').replaceAll('\n', '<br/>')
    27→}
    28→
    29→function fence(content: string): string {
    30→  const m = content.match(/`+/g)
    31→  const max = m?.reduce((a, r) => Math.max(a, r.length), 0) ?? 0
    32→  return '`'.repeat(Math.max(3, max + 1))
    33→}
    34→
    35→function walkText(node: Record<string, unknown>): string {
    36→  const children = node.children as Record<string, unknown>[] | undefined
    37→  if (!children) return (node.text as string) ?? ''
    38→  return children.map(walkText).join('\n')
    39→}
    40→
    41→function stateText(state: unknown): string {
    42→  if (!state || typeof state !== 'object') return ''
    43→  const root = (state as Record<string, unknown>).root as
    44→    | Record<string, unknown>
    45→    | undefined
    46→  return root ? walkText(root) : ''
    47→}
    48→
    49→// ── TextMatch (inline) ──
    50→
    51→export const SPOILER_TRANSFORMER: TextMatchTransformer = {
    52→  dependencies: [],
    53→  export: (node) =>
    54→    node.getType() === 'spoiler' ? `||${node.getTextContent()}||` : null,
    55→  importRegExp: /\|\|(.+?)\|\|/,
    56→  regExp: /\|\|(.+?)\|\|$/,
    57→  replace: NOOP as any,
    58→  trigger: '|',
    59→  type: 'text-match',
    60→}
    61→
    62→export const MENTION_TRANSFORMER: TextMatchTransformer = {
    63→  dependencies: [],
    64→  export: (node) => {
    65→    if (node.getType() !== 'mention') return null
    66→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
    67→    const base = `{${j.platform}@${j.handle}}`
    68→    return j.displayName ? `[${j.displayName}]${base}` : base
    69→  },
    70→  importRegExp: /(?:\[([^\]]+)\])?\{(\w+)@(\w[\w.-]*)\}/,
    71→  regExp: /(?:\[([^\]]+)\])?\{(\w+)@(\w[\w.-]*)\}$/,
    72→  replace: NOOP as any,
    73→  trigger: '}',
    74→  type: 'text-match',
    75→}
    76→
    77→export const FOOTNOTE_TRANSFORMER: TextMatchTransformer = {
    78→  dependencies: [],
    79→  export: (node) =>
    80→    node.getType() === 'footnote'
    81→      ? `[^${(node as any).__identifier ?? ''}]`
    82→      : null,
    83→  importRegExp: /\[\^(\w+)\]/,
    84→  regExp: /\[\^(\w+)\]$/,
    85→  replace: NOOP as any,
    86→  trigger: ']',
    87→  type: 'text-match',
    88→}
    89→
    90→export const KATEX_INLINE_TRANSFORMER: TextMatchTransformer = {
    91→  dependencies: [],
    92→  export: (node) =>
    93→    node.getType() === 'katex-inline'
    94→      ? `$${(node as any).__equation ?? ''}$`
    95→      : null,
    96→  importRegExp: /\$([^$\n]+)\$/,
    97→  regExp: /\$([^$\n]+)\$$/,
    98→  replace: NOOP as any,
    99→  trigger: '$',
   100→  type: 'text-match',
   101→}
   102→
   103→// ── TextFormat ──
   104→
   105→export const INSERT_TRANSFORMER: TextFormatTransformer = {
   106→  format: ['underline'],
   107→  tag: '++',
   108→  type: 'text-format',
   109→}
   110→
   111→export const SUPERSCRIPT_TRANSFORMER: TextFormatTransformer = {
   112→  format: ['superscript'],
   113→  tag: '^',
   114→  type: 'text-format',
   115→}
   116→
   117→export const SUBSCRIPT_TRANSFORMER: TextFormatTransformer = {
   118→  format: ['subscript'],
   119→  tag: '~',
   120→  type: 'text-format',
   121→}
   122→
   123→// ── Element (block) ──
   124→
   125→export const FOOTNOTE_SECTION_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   126→  dependencies: [],
   127→  export: (node) => {
   128→    if (node.getType() !== 'footnote-section') return null
   129→    const defs = (node as any).__definitions ?? {}
   130→    return Object.entries(defs)
   131→      .map(([id, content]) => `[^${id}]: ${content}`)
   132→      .join('\n')
   133→  },
   134→  regExp: NEVER,
   135→  replace: NOOP,
   136→  type: 'element',
   137→}
   138→
   139→export const CONTAINER_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   140→  dependencies: [],
   141→  export: (node) => {
   142→    const t = node.getType()
   143→    if (t === 'banner') {
   144→      const type = (node as any).__bannerType ?? 'note'
   145→      return `::: ${type}\n${node.getTextContent()}\n:::`
   146→    }
   147→    if (t === 'details') {
   148→      const summary = (node as any).__summary ?? ''
   149→      return `::: details{summary="${qA(summary)}"}\n${node.getTextContent()}\n:::`
   150→    }
   151→    return null
   152→  },
   153→  regExp: NEVER,
   154→  replace: NOOP,
   155→  type: 'element',
   156→}
   157→
   158→const ALERT_KEY: Record<string, string> = {
   159→  note: 'NOTE',
   160→  tip: 'TIP',
   161→  important: 'IMPORTANT',
   162→  warning: 'WARNING',
   163→  caution: 'CAUTION',
   164→}
   165→
   166→export const GIT_ALERT_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   167→  dependencies: [],
   168→  export: (node) => {
   169→    if (node.getType() !== 'alert-quote') return null
   170→    const key = ALERT_KEY[(node as any).__alertType ?? 'note'] ?? 'NOTE'
   171→    const lines = node.getTextContent().split('\n')
   172→    return [`> [!${key}]`, ...lines.map((l) => `> ${l}`)].join('\n')
   173→  },
   174→  regExp: NEVER,
   175→  replace: NOOP,
   176→  type: 'element',
   177→}
   178→
   179→export const KATEX_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: TextMatchTransformer = {
   180→  dependencies: [],
   181→  export: (node) =>
   182→    node.getType() === 'katex-block'
   183→      ? `$$${(node as any).__equation ?? ''}$$`
   184→      : null,
   185→  importRegExp: /\$\$([^$]+)\$\$/,
   186→  regExp: /\$\$([^$]+)\$\$$/,
   187→  replace: NOOP as any,
   188→  trigger: '$',
   189→  type: 'text-match',
   190→}
   191→
   192→export const IMAGE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   193→  dependencies: [],
   194→  export: (node) => {
   195→    if (node.getType() !== 'image') return null
   196→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   197→    const title = j.caption ? ` "${qA(j.caption)}"` : ''
   198→    return `![${j.altText || ''}](${j.src}${title})`
   199→  },
   200→  regExp: NEVER,
   201→  replace: NOOP,
   202→  type: 'element',
   203→}
   204→
   205→export const VIDEO_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   206→  dependencies: [],
   207→  export: (node) => {
   208→    if (node.getType() !== 'video') return null
   209→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   210→    const attrs = [`src="${qA(j.src)}"`]
   211→    if (j.poster) attrs.push(`poster="${qA(j.poster)}"`)
   212→    if (j.width) attrs.push(`width=${j.width}`)
   213→    if (j.height) attrs.push(`height=${j.height}`)
   214→    return `<video ${attrs.join(' ')} controls></video>`
   215→  },
   216→  regExp: NEVER,
   217→  replace: NOOP,
   218→  type: 'element',
   219→}
   220→
   221→export const CODE_BLOCK_NODE_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   222→  dependencies: [],
   223→  export: (node) => {
   224→    if (node.getType() !== 'code-block') return null
   225→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   226→    const f = fence(j.code || '')
   227→    return `${f}${j.language || ''}\n${j.code || ''}\n${f}`
   228→  },
   229→  regExp: NEVER,
   230→  replace: NOOP,
   231→  type: 'element',
   232→}
   233→
   234→export const LINK_CARD_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   235→  dependencies: [],
   236→  export: (node) => {
   237→    if (node.getType() !== 'link-card') return null
   238→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   239→    return j.title ? `[${j.title}](${j.url})` : `<${j.url}>`
   240→  },
   241→  regExp: NEVER,
   242→  replace: NOOP,
   243→  type: 'element',
   244→}
   245→
   246→export const MERMAID_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   247→  dependencies: [],
   248→  export: (node) => {
   249→    if (node.getType() !== 'mermaid') return null
   250→    const d = (node as any).__diagram ?? ''
   251→    const f = fence(d)
   252→    return `${f}mermaid\n${d}\n${f}`
   253→  },
   254→  regExp: NEVER,
   255→  replace: NOOP,
   256→  type: 'element',
   257→}
   258→
   259→export const GRID_CONTAINER_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   260→  dependencies: [],
   261→  export: (node) => {
   262→    if (node.getType() !== 'grid-container') return null
   263→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   264→    const cells = (j.cells ?? [])
   265→      .map((c: unknown) => `::: cell\n${stateText(c)}\n:::`)
   266→      .join('\n')
   267→    return `::: grid{cols=${j.cols ?? 2} gap="${qA(String(j.gap ?? '16px'))}"}\n${cells}\n:::`
   268→  },
   269→  regExp: NEVER,
   270→  replace: NOOP,
   271→  type: 'element',
   272→}
   273→
   274→export const HORIZONTAL_RULE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   275→  dependencies: [],
   276→  export: (node) => (node.getType() === 'horizontalrule' ? '---' : null),
   277→  regExp: NEVER,
   278→  replace: NOOP,
   279→  type: 'element',
   280→}
   281→
   282→export const TABLE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   283→  dependencies: [],
   284→  export: (node) => {
   285→    if (!$isTableNode(node)) return null
   286→    const rows = node.getChildren().filter($isTableRowNode)
   287→    if (rows.length === 0) return '|  |\n| --- |'
   288→    const data = rows.map((row) =>
   289→      row
   290→        .getChildren()
   291→        .filter($isTableCellNode)
   292→        .map((c) => escCell(c.getTextContent())),
   293→    )
   294→    const hdr = data[0]
   295→    const sep = hdr.map(() => '---')
   296→    return [
   297→      `| ${hdr.join(' | ')} |`,
   298→      `| ${sep.join(' | ')} |`,
   299→      ...data.slice(1).map((r) => `| ${r.join(' | ')} |`),
   300→    ].join('\n')
   301→  },
   302→  regExp: NEVER,
   303→  replace: NOOP,
   304→  type: 'element',
   305→}
   306→
   307→// ── Extension block transformers ──
   308→
   309→export const CODE_SNIPPET_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   310→  dependencies: [],
   311→  export: (node) => {
   312→    if (node.getType() !== 'code-snippet') return null
   313→    const j = node.exportJSON() as any
   314→    const body = (j.files ?? [])
   315→      .map((f: any) => {
   316→        const fc = fence(f.code || '')
   317→        return [
   318→          `::: file{name="${qA(f.filename || 'untitled')}" lang="${qA(f.language || '')}"}`,
   319→          `${fc}${f.language || ''}`,
   320→          f.code || '',
   321→          fc,
   322→          ':::',
   323→        ].join('\n')
   324→      })
   325→      .join('\n')
   326→    return `::: code-snippet\n${body}\n:::`
   327→  },
   328→  regExp: NEVER,
   329→  replace: NOOP,
   330→  type: 'element',
   331→}
   332→
   333→export const EMBED_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   334→  dependencies: [],
   335→  export: (node) =>
   336→    node.getType() === 'embed' ? `<${(node as any).__url || ''}>` : null,
   337→  regExp: NEVER,
   338→  replace: NOOP,
   339→  type: 'element',
   340→}
   341→
   342→export const GALLERY_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   343→  dependencies: [],
   344→  export: (node) => {
   345→    if (node.getType() !== 'gallery') return null
   346→    const imgs = (node.exportJSON() as any).images ?? []
   347→    return imgs.map((i: any) => `![${i.alt || ''}](${i.src || ''})`).join('\n')
   348→  },
   349→  regExp: NEVER,
   350→  replace: NOOP,
   351→  type: 'element',
   352→}
   353→
   354→export const TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
   355→  dependencies: [],
   356→  export: (node) =>
   357→    node.getType() === 'tldraw'
   358→      ? `<tldraw>\n${(node as any).__snapshot || ''}\n</tldraw>`
   359→      : null,
   360→  regExp: NEVER,
   361→  replace: NOOP,
   362→  type: 'element',
   363→}
   364→
   365→// ── Aggregate ──
   366→
   367→export const allHeadlessTransformers = [
   368→  // Inline
   369→  SPOILER_TRANSFORMER,
   370→  MENTION_TRANSFORMER,
   371→  FOOTNOTE_TRANSFORMER,
   372→  INSERT_TRANSFORMER,
   373→  SUPERSCRIPT_TRANSFORMER,
   374→  SUBSCRIPT_TRANSFORMER,
   375→  KATEX_INLINE_TRANSFORMER,
   376→  // Block (specific first)
   377→  FOOTNOTE_SECTION_TRANSFORMER,
   378→  CONTAINER_TRANSFORMER,
   379→  GIT_ALERT_TRANSFORMER,
   380→  CHECK_LIST,
   381→  KATEX_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   382→  IMAGE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   383→  VIDEO_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   384→  CODE_BLOCK_NODE_TRANSFORMER,
   385→  LINK_CARD_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   386→  MERMAID_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   387→  GRID_CONTAINER_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   388→  HORIZONTAL_RULE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   389→  TABLE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   390→  CODE_SNIPPET_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   391→  EMBED_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   392→  GALLERY_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   393→  TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER,
   394→  // Standard (heading, quote, list, code, bold, italic, strikethrough, link…)
   395→  ...TRANSFORMERS,
   396→]
   397→
   398→/** Call inside editor.read() to convert current state to Markdown. */
   399→export function $toMarkdown(): string {
   400→  return $convertToMarkdownString(allHeadlessTransformers)
   401→}
   402→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     2→import { createContext, use, useMemo } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import type { RichEditorVariant } from '../types'
     5→import type { RendererConfig } from '../types/renderer-config'
     6→
     7→export type RendererMode = 'editor' | 'renderer'
     8→
     9→interface RendererConfigContextValue {
    10→  config?: RendererConfig
    11→  mode: RendererMode
    12→  variant: RichEditorVariant
    13→}
    14→
    15→const RendererConfigContext = createContext<RendererConfigContextValue>({
    16→  config: undefined,
    17→  mode: 'renderer',
    18→  variant: 'article',
    19→})
    20→
    21→export interface RendererConfigProviderProps {
    22→  config?: RendererConfig
    23→  mode: RendererMode
    24→  variant: RichEditorVariant
    25→  children: ReactNode
    26→}
    27→
    28→export function RendererConfigProvider({
    29→  config,
    30→  mode,
    31→  variant,
    32→  children,
    33→}: RendererConfigProviderProps) {
    34→  const value = useMemo(
    35→    () => ({ config, mode, variant }),
    36→    [config, mode, variant],
    37→  )
    38→  return (
    39→    <RendererConfigContext.Provider value={value}>
    40→      {children}
    41→    </RendererConfigContext.Provider>
    42→  )
    43→}
    44→
    45→export function useRendererConfig(): RendererConfig | undefined {
    46→  return use(RendererConfigContext).config
    47→}
    48→
    49→export function useRendererMode(): RendererMode {
    50→  return use(RendererConfigContext).mode
    51→}
    52→
    53→export function useVariant(): RichEditorVariant {
    54→  return use(RendererConfigContext).variant
    55→}
    56→
```
