_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 08:45:45Z)**_

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the complete implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer`

### 1. Complete File Structure

```
packages/rich-ssr-renderer/
  package.json
  tsconfig.json
  vite.config.ts
  src/
    index.ts                          # Public API: RichSSRRenderer, preprocessFootnotes, SSRRendererConfig
    types.ts                          # All serialized node type definitions, SSRRendererConfig, RichSSRRendererProps
    preprocess.ts                     # Footnote pre-processing (DFS pass)
    render-node.tsx                   # Core recursive node renderer dispatch
    render-text.tsx                   # Text node rendering with format bitmask -> className
    render-element.tsx                # Built-in element nodes (paragraph, heading, quote, list, etc.)
    render-decorator.tsx              # Custom decorator nodes (image, code-block, katex, etc.)
    RichSSRRenderer.tsx               # Top-level component (entry point)
```

### 2. Core Rendering Pipeline

The pipeline is: **parse -> preprocess -> walk -> render**.

**2a. Parse**: The input `SerializedEditorState` JSON is already parsed. The top-level component receives it typed. No Lexical runtime is involved.

**2b. Preprocess**: A single synchronous pass calls `preprocessFootnotes(root)` which performs a DFS over the entire tree (including nested editor states in `alert-quote`, `banner`, `grid-container`), collecting footnote identifiers in document order, building a `displayNumberMap` and extracting `definitions` from any `footnote-section` nodes.

**2c. Walk**: The recursive `renderNode()` function dispatches on `node.type`. Each node maps to either an element renderer (for builtin element nodes) or a decorator renderer (for custom decorator nodes). The `renderNode` function passes through the `SSRRendererConfig` and `footnoteContext` as plain arguments (not React context, since RSC has no hooks).

**2d. Render**: Each specific renderer function returns JSX elements with the exact same CSS class names as the client-side Lexical theme.

### 3. Node Type -> JSX Output Mapping

#### Built-in Element Nodes (in `render-element.tsx`)

| Serialized `type` | HTML Output | CSS Class |
|---|---|---|
| `root` | Fragment wrapper, renders `children` | (none) |
| `paragraph` | `<p className="rich-paragraph">` | `rich-paragraph` |
| `heading` (tag: h1-h6) | `<h1-h6 className="rich-heading-h1" id={slugified}>` | `rich-heading-{tag}` |
| `quote` | `<blockquote className="rich-quote">` | `rich-quote` |
| `list` (listType: bullet) | `<ul className="rich-list-ul">` | `rich-list-ul` |
| `list` (listType: number) | `<ol className="rich-list-ol" start={start}>` | `rich-list-ol` |
| `list` (listType: check) | `<ul className="rich-list-ul rich-checklist">` | `rich-checklist` |
| `listitem` | `<li className="rich-list-item">` with checked variant | `rich-list-item` / `rich-list-item-checked` / `rich-list-item-unchecked` |
| `listitem` (nested) | `<li className="rich-list-nested-item">` | `rich-list-nested-item` |
| `link` / `autolink` | `<a href={url} className="rich-link" rel={rel} target={target}>` | `rich-link` |
| `code` (Lexical builtin) | `<pre className="rich-code-block"><code>` | `rich-code-block` |
| `table` | `<div className="rich-table-scrollable-wrapper"><table className="rich-table">` | `rich-table` |
| `tablerow` | `<tr>` | (none) |
| `tablecell` | `<td className="rich-table-cell">` or `<th className="rich-table-cell-header">` | `rich-table-cell` / `rich-table-cell-header` |
| `horizontalrule` | `<hr className="rich-hr" />` | `rich-hr` |
| `spoiler` | `<span className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>` (ElementNode with inline children) | `rich-spoiler` |
| `details` | `<details className="rich-details"><summary className="rich-details-summary"><span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span></summary><div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div></details>` | `rich-details` |

#### Built-in Leaf Nodes (in `render-text.tsx`)

| Serialized `type` | HTML Output |
|---|---|
| `text` | `<span className="{format classes}">{text}</span>` - multiple wrappings for combined formats |
| `linebreak` | `<br />` |
| `tab` | `\t` (tab character in text) |

#### Custom Decorator Nodes (in `render-decorator.tsx`)

Each decorator node type checks `SSRRendererConfig` for a custom renderer first, falling back to a built-in static SSR output.

| Serialized `type` | Renderer Config Key | Default SSR Output |
|---|---|---|
| `image` | `Image` | `<div className="rich-image-wrapper"><figure className="rich-image"><img src={src} alt={altText} width={width} height={height} loading="lazy" />{caption && <figcaption>{caption}</figcaption>}</figure></div>` |
| `video` | `Video` | `<div className="rich-video-wrapper"><figure className="rich-video"><video src={src} poster={poster} controls preload="metadata" /></figure></div>` |
| `code-block` | `CodeBlock` | `<div className="rich-code-block-wrapper"><div className="rich-code-block">{header}<pre><code>{code}</code></pre></div></div>` |
| `katex-inline` | `KaTeX` | `<span><code className="rich-katex-fallback">{equation}</code></span>` (actual KaTeX rendering is client-side; SSR shows raw formula) |
| `katex-block` | `KaTeX` | `<div className="rich-katex-block-wrapper"><code className="rich-katex-fallback">{equation}</code></div>` |
| `mermaid` | `Mermaid` | `<div className="rich-mermaid-wrapper"><div className="rich-mermaid-block"><pre><code>{diagram}</code></pre></div></div>` |
| `mention` | `Mention` | `<span className="rich-mention rich-mention-plain"><span className="rich-mention-handle">@{displayName or handle}</span></span>` |
| `link-card` | `LinkCard` | `<div className="rich-link-card-wrapper"><a className="rich-link-card" href={url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">...</a></div>` |
| `footnote` | `Footnote` | `<sup className="rich-footnote"><span className="rich-footnote-ref-wrapper"><a className="rich-footnote-ref" href="#footnote-{id}" id="footnote-ref-{id}" role="doc-noteref">{displayNumber}</a></span></sup>` |
| `footnote-section` | `FootnoteSection` | `<div className="rich-footnote-section"><div className="rich-footnote-section-content" role="doc-endnotes"><hr className="rich-footnote-section-divider" /><ol className="rich-footnote-section-list">...</ol></div></div>` |
| `alert-quote` | `Alert` (header only) | `<div className="rich-alert rich-alert-{type}"><div className="rich-alert-header rich-alert-header-{type}">...</div><div className="rich-alert-content">{recursively rendered content}</div></div>` |
| `banner` | `Banner` (icon only) | `<div className="rich-banner rich-banner-{type}"><div className="rich-banner-inner"><span className="rich-banner-icon rich-banner-icon-{type}" /><div className="rich-banner-content">{recursively rendered content}</div></div></div>` |
| `grid-container` | (none) | `<div className="rich-grid-container"><div className="rich-grid-inner" style={{display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat({cols}, 1fr)', gap: '{gap}'}}>{cells mapped to divs}</div></div>` |
| `spoiler` | (none) | Already handled as element node |
| `details` | (none) | Already handled as element node |
| `embed` | (none) | `<div><a href={url}>{url}</a></div>` (graceful fallback) |
| `code-snippet` | `CodeSnippet` | Render each file's code block with filename header |
| `gallery` | `Gallery` | `<div className="rich-gallery">{images.map(img => <img>)}</div>` |
| `tldraw` | (none) | `<div>[Tldraw content]</div>` (skip or placeholder) |

### 4. Footnote Pre-Processing Algorithm

```typescript
// In preprocess.ts

interface FootnoteContext {
  displayNumberMap: Record<string, number>
  definitions: Record<string, string>
}

function preprocessFootnotes(root: SerializedRootNode): FootnoteContext {
  const seen = new Set<string>()
  const numberMap: Record<string, number> = {}
  let counter = 1
  let definitions: Record<string, string> = {}

  function dfs(node: SerializedAnyNode): void {
    // Check if this is a footnote node
    if (node.type === 'footnote') {
      const id = (node as SerializedFootnoteNode).identifier
      if (!seen.has(id)) {
        seen.add(id)
        numberMap[id] = counter++
      }
    }

    // Check if this is a footnote-section node (collect definitions)
    if (node.type === 'footnote-section') {
      const defs = (node as SerializedFootnoteSectionNode).definitions
      if (defs) {
        definitions = { ...definitions, ...defs }
      }
    }

    // Recurse into children (element nodes)
    if ('children' in node && Array.isArray(node.children)) {
      for (const child of node.children) {
        dfs(child)
      }
    }

    // Recurse into nested editor states
    if (node.type === 'alert-quote' || node.type === 'banner') {
      const content = (node as any).content as SerializedEditorState | undefined
      if (content?.root?.children) {
        for (const child of content.root.children) {
          dfs(child)
        }
      }
    }

    if (node.type === 'grid-container') {
      const cells = (node as any).cells as SerializedEditorState[] | undefined
      if (cells) {
        for (const cell of cells) {
          if (cell?.root?.children) {
            for (const child of cell.root.children) {
              dfs(child)
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  // Walk all top-level children
  for (const child of root.children) {
    dfs(child)
  }

  return { displayNumberMap: numberMap, definitions }
}
```

Key design decisions:
- Single DFS pass before any rendering begins
- Traverses into nested `SerializedEditorState` (alert-quote, banner, grid-container)
- Assigns sequential display numbers, deduplicating by identifier (same logic as `FootnotePlugin`)
- Collects definitions from `footnote-section` nodes

### 5. Renderer Config Injection Mechanism

Since React Server Components cannot use hooks or context, the config is passed as **props** through the rendering tree.

```typescript
// In types.ts

export interface SSRRendererConfig {
  Alert?: ComponentType<AlertRendererProps>
  Banner?: ComponentType<BannerRendererProps>
  CodeBlock?: ComponentType<CodeBlockRendererProps>
  CodeSnippet?: ComponentType<CodeSnippetRendererProps>
  Footnote?: ComponentType<SSRFootnoteRendererProps>    // SSR variant (no tooltip)
  FootnoteSection?: ComponentType<SSRFootnoteSectionRendererProps>
  Gallery?: ComponentType<GalleryRendererProps>
  Image?: ComponentType<ImageRendererProps>
  KaTeX?: ComponentType<KaTeXRendererProps>
  LinkCard?: ComponentType<LinkCardRendererProps>
  Mermaid?: ComponentType<MermaidRendererProps>
  Mention?: ComponentType<MentionRendererProps>
  Video?: ComponentType<VideoRendererProps>
}

// Internal rendering context (plain object, not React context)
interface RenderContext {
  rendererConfig?: SSRRendererConfig
  footnoteContext: FootnoteContext
  unknownNodeStrategy: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug'
}
```

The `RenderContext` object is threaded through every `renderNode()` call as a plain function argument:

```typescript
function renderNode(node: SerializedAnyNode, ctx: RenderContext, key: string | number): ReactNode
```

For the top-level component:

```typescript
interface RichSSRRendererProps {
  value: SerializedEditorState
  variant?: RichEditorVariant      // 'article' | 'note' | 'comment'
  theme?: 'light' | 'dark'
  className?: string
  style?: CSSProperties
  as?: ElementType
  rendererConfig?: SSRRendererConfig
  unknownNodeStrategy?: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug'
}
```

### 6. Nested Editor State Handling

Three node types contain nested `SerializedEditorState`: `alert-quote` (field: `content`), `banner` (field: `content`), `grid-container` (field: `cells: SerializedEditorState[]`).

The approach is to call a shared helper `renderEditorState()` that takes a `SerializedEditorState` and the `RenderContext`, renders `state.root.children` by mapping each child through `renderNode()`:

```typescript
// In render-node.tsx

function renderEditorState(
  state: SerializedEditorState,
  ctx: RenderContext,
): ReactNode {
  if (!state?.root?.children) return null
  return state.root.children.map((child, i) => renderNode(child, ctx, i))
}
```

For alert-quote:
```tsx
<div className={`rich-alert rich-alert-${alertType}`}>
  {AlertComponent ? <AlertComponent type={alertType} /> : <DefaultAlertHeader type={alertType} />}
  <div className="rich-alert-content">
    {renderEditorState(node.content, ctx)}
  </div>
</div>
```

For banner:
```tsx
<div className={`rich-banner rich-banner-${bannerType}`}>
  <div className="rich-banner-inner">
    {BannerComponent ? <BannerComponent type={bannerType} /> : <DefaultBannerIcon type={bannerType} />}
    <div className="rich-banner-content">
      {renderEditorState(node.content, ctx)}
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

For grid-container:
```tsx
<div className="rich-grid-container">
  <div className="rich-grid-inner" style={{ display: 'grid', gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, 1fr)`, gap }}>
    {cells.map((cellState, i) => (
      <div key={i} className="rich-grid-cell">
        {renderEditorState(cellState, ctx)}
      </div>
    ))}
  </div>
</div>
```

The key insight: the same `RenderContext` (including `footnoteContext` and `rendererConfig`) is passed into nested state rendering, so footnotes inside nested states are correctly numbered using the pre-computed `displayNumberMap`.

### 7. Text Format Bitmask -> className Mapping

From the Lexical source, the text format bitmask values are:

```typescript
// In render-text.tsx

const FORMAT_BOLD = 1
const FORMAT_ITALIC = 2
const FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH = 4
const FORMAT_UNDERLINE = 8
const FORMAT_CODE = 16
const FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT = 32
const FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT = 64
const FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT = 128

const FORMAT_CLASS_MAP: [number, string][] = [
  [FORMAT_BOLD, 'rich-text-bold'],
  [FORMAT_ITALIC, 'rich-text-italic'],
  [FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH, 'rich-text-strikethrough'],
  [FORMAT_UNDERLINE, 'rich-text-underline'],
  [FORMAT_CODE, 'rich-text-code'],
  [FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT, 'rich-text-subscript'],
  [FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT, 'rich-text-superscript'],
  [FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT, 'rich-text-highlight'],
]

function getTextFormatClasses(format: number): string {
  const classes: string[] = []
  for (const [mask, cls] of FORMAT_CLASS_MAP) {
    if (format & mask) classes.push(cls)
  }
  return classes.join(' ')
}
```

The text node renderer:

```tsx
function renderTextNode(node: SerializedTextNode, key: string | number): ReactNode {
  if (node.type === 'linebreak') return <br key={key} />
  if (node.type === 'tab') return '\t'

  const text = node.text
  const format = node.format || 0

  if (format === 0) return text

  const className = getTextFormatClasses(format)
  return <span key={key} className={className}>{text}</span>
}
```

This matches the Lexical theme exactly because the `editorTheme` in `theme.ts` defines:
- `text.bold` -> `rich-text-bold`
- `text.italic` -> `rich-text-italic`
- etc.

When multiple formats are combined (e.g., bold + italic), Lexical applies all class names to a single `<span>`. We replicate this by joining all matching class names.

### 8. Error Handling for Malformed Input

Three strategies, controlled by the `unknownNodeStrategy` prop (default: `'skip'`):

```typescript
function renderUnknownNode(
  node: SerializedAnyNode,
  strategy: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug',
  key: string | number,
): ReactNode {
  switch (strategy) {
    case 'skip':
      return null

    case 'text':
      // Attempt to extract text content from children
      if ('children' in node && Array.isArray(node.children)) {
        return walkText(node)
      }
      if ('text' in node && typeof node.text === 'string') {
        return node.text
      }
      return null

    case 'debug':
      return (
        <div
          key={key}
          style={{
            border: '1px dashed red',
            padding: '4px',
            fontSize: '12px',
            fontFamily: 'monospace',
          }}
        >
          Unknown node: {node.type}
        </div>
      )
  }
}
```

Additional safeguards:
- The top-level `RichSSRRenderer` wraps rendering in a try-catch; on failure, returns `null` and logs a warning
- Individual node renderers gracefully handle missing/malformed properties with defaults
- `preprocessFootnotes` is fault-tolerant: missing `children` arrays are treated as empty

### 9. Integration Point with the Web App

The current rendering chain in the web app is:

```
LexicalContent.tsx ('use client')
  -> IsolateRenderer (Shadow DOM wrapper)
    -> ShiroRenderer
      -> RichRenderer (uses LexicalComposer)
```

The SSR integration adds a new path that **replaces this entire chain for server rendering**, while keeping the client renderer for hydration / interactive features.

**Option A: Direct replacement for read-only pages (recommended)**

For pages that only display content (post detail, note detail, pages), the web app can use the SSR renderer directly in Server Components without any `'use client'` directive:

```tsx
// In a Server Component (e.g., apps/web/src/app/(main)/posts/[slug]/page.tsx)
import { RichSSRRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer'
import { ssrRendererConfig } from './ssr-config' // app-level SSR renderers

// This renders real HTML on the server - no empty contentEditable shells
<RichSSRRenderer
  value={editorState}
  variant="article"
  className="rich-content"
  rendererConfig={ssrRendererConfig}
/>
```

**Option B: Hybrid approach (SSR + client hydration)**

For pages that need interactive features (spoiler click, footnote tooltip), render SSR HTML for initial paint, then hydrate with the client renderer:

```tsx
// Server component renders visible HTML
<Suspense fallback={<RichSSRRenderer value={editorState} variant="article" />}>
  <LexicalContent content={serialized} variant="article" />
</Suspense>
```

**Integration into `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`**

Add a new export path to `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`:

```
"./ssr": "./src/ssr.tsx"
```

This `ssr.tsx` would create a `ShiroSSRRenderer` that wraps `RichSSRRenderer` with the same `enhancedRendererConfig` adapted for SSR (using the SSR-compatible subset of renderers).

### 10. Phased Implementation Order

**Phase 1: Foundation (files: `types.ts`, `render-text.tsx`, `render-element.tsx`)**
- Define all serialized node type interfaces in `types.ts`
- Implement text format bitmask rendering
- Implement all built-in element nodes (paragraph, heading, quote, list, link, table, hr, code, spoiler, details)
- Result: Can render any document that uses only built-in Lexical nodes

**Phase 2: Core decorator nodes (`render-decorator.tsx`, `preprocess.ts`)**
- Implement simple decorator nodes: image, video, code-block, mention, link-card, mermaid, katex (inline/block)
- Implement footnote pre-processing
- Implement footnote and footnote-section rendering
- Result: Can render most real-world documents

**Phase 3: Nested editor states (additions to `render-decorator.tsx`)**
- Implement alert-quote with recursive `renderEditorState`
- Implement banner with recursive `renderEditorState`
- Implement grid-container with recursive `renderEditorState` over cells array
- Result: Full nested content support

**Phase 4: Renderer config + top-level component (`RichSSRRenderer.tsx`, `index.ts`)**
- Wire up `SSRRendererConfig` prop threading
- Implement `RichSSRRenderer` top-level component with variant class, error handling
- Implement unknown node strategy
- Set up package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json
- Result: Complete standalone package

**Phase 5: Extension nodes**
- Implement embed, code-snippet, gallery, tldraw (placeholder) rendering
- Handle legacy serialized formats (banner with `children` instead of `content`)

**Phase 6: Integration (`@haklex/rich-kit-shiro` changes)**
- Add `./ssr` export to `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Create `ShiroSSRRenderer` that wires up `enhancedRendererConfig` (SSR-compatible subset)
- Update `LexicalContent.tsx` in web app to use SSR renderer for initial render

**Phase 7: Testing + Polish**
- Verify CSS class name parity with client renderer
- Test all node types with real serialized data from the blog
- Verify footnote numbering matches client-side behavior
- Test edge cases: empty documents, deeply nested structures, malformed JSON

---

### Package Configuration Details

**package.json** (following `@haklex/rich-headless` pattern):

```json
{
  "name": "@haklex/rich-ssr-renderer",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "Server-side renderer for Lexical serialized state (zero Lexical runtime)",
  "license": "MIT",
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    ".": "./src/index.ts"
  },
  "main": "./src/index.ts",
  "files": ["dist"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev:build": "vite build --watch"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/react": "^19.2.14",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "vite": "^7.3.1",
    "vite-plugin-dts": "^4.5.4"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "react": ">=19"
  },
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public",
    "exports": {
      ".": {
        "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts"
      }
    },
    "main": "./dist/index.mjs",
    "types": "./dist/index.d.ts"
  }
}
```

Key: zero `dependencies` section. Only `react` as peer dep. No Lexical, no browser APIs.

**vite.config.ts**:

```typescript
import { createViteConfig } from '../vite.shared'

export default createViteConfig({
  vanillaExtract: false,
  esbuild: { jsx: 'automatic', jsxImportSource: 'react' },
})
```

**tsconfig.json** (following rich-headless, but adding JSX support):

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2021",
    "lib": ["ES2021"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "outDir": "dist",
    "jsx": "react-jsx"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Note: `lib` does NOT include `"DOM"` -- this enforces the zero-browser-API constraint at compile time. Any accidental `document` or `window` reference will be a type error.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/styles/theme.ts` - Source of truth for all CSS class names that the SSR renderer must replicate exactly
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/types/renderer-config.ts` - Defines `RendererConfig` interface and all renderer prop types; the SSR equivalent (`SSRRendererConfig`) must mirror these keys
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx` - Contains the canonical footnote numbering algorithm (DFS over `FootnoteNode` instances, deduplication, sequential counter) that must be replicated for serialized JSON
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts` - Defines all node type strings and their property shapes (the headless decorator definitions serve as the canonical reference for which properties each serialized node type carries)
- `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/ShiroRenderer.tsx` - Integration point pattern; the SSR equivalent (`ShiroSSRRenderer`) will follow this same structure of composing `enhancedRendererConfig` with the base renderer

```md
# @haklex/rich-ssr-renderer 实施计划

## Context

`RichRenderer`（`@haklex/rich-editor`）依赖 `LexicalComposer`，SSR 阶段输出空 `contentEditable` 壳，无正文 HTML，损 SEO 与首屏体验。需新建纯 React 包，直接遍历 `SerializedEditorState` JSON 输出语义 HTML，零 Lexical 运行时，零浏览器 API，兼容 Next.js Server Component。

## 文件结构

\```
packages/rich-ssr-renderer/
  package.json
  tsconfig.json          # lib: ["ES2021"] 不含 "DOM"，编译期禁止 window/document
  vite.config.ts         # vanillaExtract: false, jsx: automatic
  src/
    index.ts             # 公开导出: RichSSRRenderer, SSRRendererConfig, preprocessFootnotes
    types.ts             # 序列化节点类型定义 + SSRRendererConfig + Props
    preprocess.ts        # 脚注预处理（DFS 收集 displayNumberMap + definitions）
    render-node.tsx      # 核心递归分派: renderNode(node, ctx, key) -> ReactNode
    render-text.tsx      # text/linebreak/tab 节点 + format bitmask -> className
    render-element.tsx   # 内置元素节点: paragraph/heading/quote/list/link/table/code/spoiler/details
    render-decorator.tsx # 自定义装饰节点: image/video/code-block/katex/mention/footnote/alert/banner/grid 等
    RichSSRRenderer.tsx  # 顶层组件：解析 -> 预处理 -> 渲染
\```

## 核心 API

\```typescript
interface RichSSRRendererProps {
  value: SerializedEditorState | string   // JSON 对象或字符串
  variant?: 'article' | 'note' | 'comment'
  theme?: 'light' | 'dark'
  className?: string
  style?: CSSProperties
  as?: ElementType                        // 默认 'div'
  rendererConfig?: SSRRendererConfig      // 自定义渲染器覆盖
  unknownNodeStrategy?: 'skip' | 'text' | 'debug'  // 默认 'skip'
}
\```

`SSRRendererConfig` 键名与现有 `RendererConfig` 一致：Alert / Banner / CodeBlock / CodeSnippet / Footnote / FootnoteSection / Gallery / Image / KaTeX / LinkCard / Mermaid / Mention / Video。

## 渲染管线

\```
parse(value) -> preprocessFootnotes(root) -> renderChildren(root.children, ctx) -> ReactNode
\```

1. **Parse**：若 `value` 为 string 则 `JSON.parse`，校验 `root.children` 存在
2. **Preprocess**：单次 DFS 收集脚注（含嵌套 editor state），构建 `{ displayNumberMap, definitions }`
3. **Walk**：`renderNode()` 按 `node.type` 分派至 element / text / decorator 渲染函数
4. **Config inject**：`RenderContext` 为普通对象（非 React context），逐层传递 `rendererConfig` + `footnoteContext` + `unknownNodeStrategy`

## Text Format Bitmask

\```typescript
const FORMAT_MAP: [number, string][] = [
  [1,   'rich-text-bold'],
  [2,   'rich-text-italic'],
  [4,   'rich-text-strikethrough'],
  [8,   'rich-text-underline'],
  [16,  'rich-text-code'],
  [32,  'rich-text-subscript'],
  [64,  'rich-text-superscript'],
  [128, 'rich-text-highlight'],
]
\```

多格式合并为单 `<span className="rich-text-bold rich-text-italic">`，与 Lexical theme 输出一致。

## 节点映射

### 内置元素节点（render-element.tsx）

| type | HTML | class |
|------|------|-------|
| paragraph | `<p>` | `rich-paragraph` |
| heading | `<h1>`-`<h6>` + `id={slug}` | `rich-heading-{tag}` |
| quote | `<blockquote>` | `rich-quote` |
| list (bullet) | `<ul>` | `rich-list-ul` |
| list (number) | `<ol start={start}>` | `rich-list-ol` |
| list (check) | `<ul>` | `rich-list-ul rich-checklist` |
| listitem | `<li>` | `rich-list-item` / `rich-list-item-checked` / `rich-list-item-unchecked` |
| listitem (nested) | `<li>` | `rich-list-nested-item` |
| link / autolink | `<a href rel target>` | `rich-link` |
| code | `<pre><code>` | `rich-code-block` |
| table | `<div><table>` | `rich-table-scrollable-wrapper` / `rich-table` |
| tablerow | `<tr>` | — |
| tablecell | `<td>` / `<th>` | `rich-table-cell` / `rich-table-cell-header` |
| horizontalrule | `<hr />` | `rich-hr` |
| spoiler | `<span role="button" tabIndex={0}>` | `rich-spoiler` |
| details | `<details><summary>` | `rich-details` / `rich-details-summary` / `rich-details-content` |

### 自定义装饰节点（render-decorator.tsx）

| type | config key | SSR 默认输出 |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| image | Image | `<figure><img src alt width height loading="lazy" /><figcaption></figure>` |
| video | Video | `<figure><video src poster controls preload="metadata" /></figure>` |
| code-block | CodeBlock | `<div><div class="rich-code-block"><pre><code>{code}</code></pre></div></div>` |
| katex-inline | KaTeX | `<span><code>{equation}</code></span>`（SSR 降级为公式文本） |
| katex-block | KaTeX | `<div><code>{equation}</code></div>` |
| mermaid | Mermaid | `<div><pre><code>{diagram}</code></pre></div>` |
| mention | Mention | `<span class="rich-mention">@{displayName \|\| handle}</span>` |
| link-card | LinkCard | `<a class="rich-link-card" href target="_blank">title + description</a>` |
| footnote | Footnote | `<sup><a class="rich-footnote-ref" href="#footnote-{id}">{displayNumber}</a></sup>` |
| footnote-section | FootnoteSection | `<hr /><ol>{sorted items with backlinks}</ol>` |
| alert-quote | Alert | `<div class="rich-alert rich-alert-{type}">{header}{renderEditorState(content)}</div>` |
| banner | Banner | `<div class="rich-banner rich-banner-{type}">{icon}{renderEditorState(content)}</div>` |
| grid-container | — | `<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(cols,1fr); gap">{cells}</div>` |
| embed | — | `<a href={url}>{url}</a>`（链接降级） |
| code-snippet | CodeSnippet | 逐文件渲染 code-block |
| gallery | Gallery | `<div>{images.map(img => <img>)}</div>` |
| tldraw | — | `<div>[Tldraw drawing]</div>`（占位） |

## 脚注预处理算法（preprocess.ts）

\```typescript
function preprocessFootnotes(root): FootnoteContext {
  const seen = new Set<string>()
  const numberMap: Record<string, number> = {}
  let counter = 1
  let definitions: Record<string, string> = {}

  function dfs(node) {
    if (node.type === 'footnote' && !seen.has(node.identifier)) {
      seen.add(node.identifier)
      numberMap[node.identifier] = counter++
    }
    if (node.type === 'footnote-section') {
      definitions = { ...definitions, ...node.definitions }
    }
    // 递归 children
    if (node.children) node.children.forEach(dfs)
    // 递归嵌套 editor state (alert-quote, banner)
    if (node.content?.root?.children) node.content.root.children.forEach(dfs)
    // 递归 grid cells
    if (node.cells) node.cells.forEach(cell => cell?.root?.children?.forEach(dfs))
  }

  root.children.forEach(dfs)
  return { displayNumberMap: numberMap, definitions }
}
\```

对齐 `FootnotePlugin.tsx:19` 逻辑：DFS 顺序遍历，去重，顺序编号。

## 嵌套 Editor State 递归

`renderEditorState(state, ctx)` 调用 `state.root.children.map(child => renderNode(child, ctx, i))`，同一 `RenderContext` 贯穿——脚注编号全局一致。

## 未知节点策略

- `skip`：返回 null
- `text`：尝试提取 `children` 或 `text` 字段的文本内容
- `debug`：渲染红色虚线框 + 节点类型名

顶层 `RichSSRRenderer` 包裹 try-catch，解析/渲染失败返回 null 并 console.warn。

## 包配置

**package.json** — 零 dependencies，仅 `react>=19` 为 peer dep。无 Lexical 依赖。

**tsconfig.json** — `lib: ["ES2021"]` 不含 `"DOM"`，编译期杜绝 `window`/`document` 引用。

**vite.config.ts** — 复用 `packages/vite.shared.ts`，`vanillaExtract: false`。

## 分阶段执行

### Phase 1：脚手架 + 文本渲染
- 创建包结构（package.json / tsconfig.json / vite.config.ts）
- `types.ts`：定义所有序列化节点接口 + `SSRRendererConfig` + `RichSSRRendererProps` + `RenderContext`
- `render-text.tsx`：text format bitmask -> className，linebreak -> `<br/>`
- `render-element.tsx`：paragraph / heading / quote / list / listitem / link / table / hr / spoiler / details
- `render-node.tsx`：分派骨架（element + text）
- `RichSSRRenderer.tsx`：最小可用——parse + renderChildren
- `index.ts`：导出
- **验证**：能渲染纯文本 + 标题 + 列表 + 链接文档

### Phase 2：核心装饰节点 + 脚注
- `preprocess.ts`：脚注预处理 DFS
- `render-decorator.tsx`：image / video / code-block / mention / katex-inline / katex-block / mermaid / link-card / footnote / footnote-section
- 接入 `RichSSRRenderer` 预处理流程
- **验证**：能渲染含图片、代码块、脚注的真实文章

### Phase 3：嵌套容器节点
- alert-quote / banner：递归 `renderEditorState(content, ctx)`
- grid-container：遍历 `cells[]` 递归渲染
- legacy 格式兼容（banner `children` -> `content`）
- **验证**：能渲染含 alert/banner/grid 的复杂文档

### Phase 4：扩展节点 + 配置系统
- embed / code-snippet / gallery / tldraw 静态降级
- `SSRRendererConfig` 覆盖机制完整接入
- `unknownNodeStrategy` 三策略实现
- **验证**：全节点类型覆盖，自定义 renderer 可注入

### Phase 5：集成
- `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro` 新增 `./ssr` 导出，创建 `ShiroSSRRenderer`
- Web app `LexicalContent.server.tsx`（Server Component 版）
- 现有客户端 `LexicalContent.tsx` 保留为 fallback / 交互增强层
- **验证**：`renderToString` 输出含正文 HTML，非空壳

## 关键文件引用

| 用途 | 路径 |
|------|------|
| CSS class 名源 | `packages/rich-editor/src/styles/theme.ts` |
| RendererConfig 类型 | `packages/rich-editor/src/types/renderer-config.ts` |
| 脚注算法源 | `packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx` |
| 节点属性规范 | `packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts` |
| 共享 Vite 配置 | `packages/vite.shared.ts` |
| Web 消费端 | `apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx` |
| Kit 集成层 | `packages/rich-kit-shiro/` |

## 验证方案

1. `renderToString(<RichSSRRenderer value={realData} />)` 输出含正文文本（非空 contentEditable）
2. 每类节点至少 1 个快照用例
3. 脚注编号：重复引用同 id / definition 缺失 / definition 多余 三场景
4. 嵌套：banner 内含 list + code + link
5. 异常输入：非法 JSON / 无 root / 未知 node
6. 静态检查：源码无 `window` / `document` / `navigator`（tsconfig 不含 DOM lib 保证）
7. 构建产物不引入 `@lexical/*`

     RichRenderer.tsx:43)。
     建议：要么明确“不兼容现有 enhancedRendererConfig”，要么在 SSR 包里补齐对应 Provider 契约。
  4. Medium：脚注行为与现有实现可能不一致。现有逻辑会在有 footnote 但缺 section 时自动补 section；计划未明确保留该语义。参考
     FootnotePlugin.tsx (<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx:46)。
     建议：默认启用“缺失时合成 footnote section”。
  5. Medium：标题 slug 去重未明确。现有实现有去重，避免重复 id。参考 HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx (<REPO_ROOT>/
     packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx:20)。
     建议：SSR 也实现相同去重策略，保证锚点稳定。
  6. Medium：details 与 spoiler 的语义保真不足。details.open 当前是持久字段；spoiler 当前依赖 click/keydown 切换，纯 SSR 若不处理会在
     触屏端不可读。参考 DetailsNode.ts (<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/DetailsNode.ts:63)、
     SpoilerNode.ts (<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/SpoilerNode.ts:24)、shared.css.ts (/Users/
     innei/git/innei-repo/Shiroi/packages/rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts:404)。
     建议：details 输出 open；spoiler 默认 SSR 可读（如默认 revealed 或可访问降级）。
```

 `./packages/rich-editor/src/components/RichRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import { PortalThemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     2→import { LexicalComposer } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposer'
     3→import { ContentEditable } from '@lexical/react/LexicalContentEditable'
     4→import { LexicalErrorBoundary } from '@lexical/react/LexicalErrorBoundary'
     5→import { RichTextPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalRichTextPlugin'
     6→
     7→import { allNodes } from '../config'
     8→import { ColorSchemeProvider } from '../context/ColorSchemeContext'
     9→import { RendererConfigProvider } from '../context/RendererConfigContext'
    10→import { FootnotePlugin } from '../plugins/FootnotePlugin'
    11→import { HeadingAnchorPlugin } from '../plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin'
    12→import { editorTheme } from '../styles/theme'
    13→import type { RichRendererProps } from '../types'
    14→import { clsx, getVariantClass } from './utils'
    15→
    16→export function RichRenderer({
    17→  value,
    18→  variant = 'article',
    19→  theme = 'light',
    20→  className,
    21→  style,
    22→  as: Component = 'div',
    23→  rendererConfig,
    24→  extraNodes,
    25→}: RichRendererProps) {
    26→  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, theme)
    27→
    28→  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
    29→
    30→  const initialConfig = {
    31→    namespace: 'RichRenderer',
    32→    theme: editorTheme,
    33→    nodes,
    34→    editable: false,
    35→    editorState: JSON.stringify(value),
    36→    onError: (error: Error) => {
    37→      console.error('[RichRenderer]', error)
    38→    },
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  return (
    42→    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass}>
    43→      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
    44→        <RendererConfigProvider
    45→          config={rendererConfig}
    46→          mode="renderer"
    47→          variant={variant}
    48→        >
    49→          <Component
    50→            className={clsx('rich-content', variantClass, className)}
    51→            style={style}
    52→            data-theme={theme}
    53→          >
    54→            <LexicalComposer initialConfig={initialConfig}>
    55→              <FootnotePlugin>
    56→                <RichTextPlugin
    57→                  contentEditable={
    58→                    <ContentEditable
    59→                      className="rich-content__body"
    60→                      style={{ outline: 'none' }}
    61→                      aria-placeholder=""
    62→                      placeholder={<span style={{ display: 'none' }} />}
    63→                    />
    64→                  }
    65→                  ErrorBoundary={LexicalErrorBoundary}
    66→                />
    67→                <HeadingAnchorPlugin />
    68→              </FootnotePlugin>
    69→            </LexicalComposer>
    70→          </Component>
    71→        </RendererConfigProvider>
    72→      </ColorSchemeProvider>
    73→    </PortalThemeProvider>
    74→  )
    75→}
    76→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 09:41:20Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/components/utils.ts`

```text
     1→import type { ColorScheme } from '../context/ColorSchemeContext'
     2→import { articleVariant, darkArticleVariant } from '../styles/article.css'
     3→import { commentVariant, darkCommentVariant } from '../styles/comment.css'
     4→import { darkNoteVariant, noteVariant } from '../styles/note.css'
     5→import type { RichEditorVariant } from '../types'
     6→
     7→export function clsx(
     8→  ...args: Array<string | undefined | null | false>
     9→): string {
    10→  return args.filter(Boolean).join(' ')
    11→}
    12→
    13→export function getVariantClass(
    14→  variant: RichEditorVariant,
    15→  colorScheme: ColorScheme,
    16→): string {
    17→  if (variant === 'comment') {
    18→    return colorScheme === 'dark' ? darkCommentVariant : commentVariant
    19→  }
    20→  if (variant === 'note') {
    21→    return colorScheme === 'dark' ? darkNoteVariant : noteVariant
    22→  }
    23→  return colorScheme === 'dark' ? darkArticleVariant : articleVariant
    24→}
    25→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 09:41:32Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see the core issue. The shared.css.ts uses `globalStyle` with `${richContent}` as the parent selector, where `richContent` is a vanilla-extract generated class name. This means ALL the `.rich-*` styles are scoped under this generated class name. 

Let me also check the variant files to understand the full CSS class hierarchy.

The key insight from the feedback:
1. **High - Variant class**: All styles are scoped under `${richContent}` (vanilla-extract generated hash). Without this root class, none of the `.rich-*` child styles apply. The SSR renderer MUST output this class on the root element.


2. **High - tsconfig DOM**: React JSX types depend on DOM types. Can't remove DOM lib.
3. **High - RendererConfig compatibility**: Enhanced renderers use hooks (useVariant, useColorScheme). Can't just reuse them.
4. **Medium - Footnote section auto-creation**: Need to synthesize section when footnotes exist but section doesn't.
5. **Medium - Heading slug dedup**: Need to track seen slugs.
6. **Medium - Details/Spoiler**: Need to handle `open` attr and spoiler accessibility.

Now I need to dig into the variant CSS files and HeadingAnchorPlugin to see how these constraints actually play out.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/HeadingAnchorPlugin.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     2→import { HeadingNode } from '@lexical/rich-text'
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→
     5→function textToSlug(text: string): string {
     6→  return (
     7→    text
     8→      .toLowerCase()
     9→      .trim()
    10→      // eslint-disable-next-line regexp/no-dupe-characters-character-class
    11→      .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
    12→      .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
    13→      .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    14→  )
    15→}
    16→
    17→function deduplicateSlug(slug: string, usedSlugs: Map<string, number>): string {
    18→  const count = usedSlugs.get(slug)
    19→  if (count === undefined) {
    20→    usedSlugs.set(slug, 1)
    21→    return slug
    22→  }
    23→  usedSlugs.set(slug, count + 1)
    24→  return `${slug}-${count}`
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function HeadingAnchorPlugin() {
    28→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    29→
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    const usedSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    32→
    33→    return editor.registerMutationListener(HeadingNode, (mutations) => {
    34→      usedSlugs.clear()
    35→
    36→      for (const [nodeKey, mutation] of mutations) {
    37→        if (mutation === 'destroyed') continue
    38→
    39→        const dom = editor.getElementByKey(nodeKey)
    40→        if (!dom) continue
    41→
    42→        const text = dom.textContent || ''
    43→        const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    44→        if (!baseSlug) continue
    45→
    46→        const slug = deduplicateSlug(baseSlug, usedSlugs)
    47→        dom.id = slug
    48→
    49→        let anchor = dom.querySelector<HTMLAnchorElement>(
    50→          '.rich-heading-anchor',
    51→        )
    52→        if (!anchor) {
    53→          anchor = document.createElement('a')
    54→          anchor.className = 'rich-heading-anchor'
    55→          anchor.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')
    56→          anchor.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1')
    57→          dom.prepend(anchor)
    58→        }
    59→        anchor.href = `#${slug}`
    60→      }
    61→    })
    62→  }, [editor])
    63→
    64→  return null
    65→}
    66→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/SpoilerNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalNode,
     4→  NodeKey,
     5→  SerializedElementNode,
     6→} from 'lexical'
     7→import { ElementNode } from 'lexical'
     8→
     9→export class SpoilerNode extends ElementNode {
    10→  static getType(): string {
    11→    return 'spoiler'
    12→  }
    13→
    14→  static clone(node: SpoilerNode): SpoilerNode {
    15→    return new SpoilerNode(node.__key)
    16→  }
    17→
    18→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
    19→    super(key)
    20→  }
    21→
    22→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    23→    const span = document.createElement('span')
    24→    span.className = 'rich-spoiler'
    25→    span.setAttribute('role', 'button')
    26→    span.setAttribute('tabindex', '0')
    27→    span.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Spoiler (click to reveal)')
    28→
    29→    const toggle = () => {
    30→      if (span.isContentEditable) return
    31→      const revealed = span.classList.toggle('rich-spoiler-revealed')
    32→      span.setAttribute(
    33→        'aria-label',
    34→        revealed ? 'Spoiler (revealed)' : 'Spoiler (click to reveal)',
    35→      )
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    span.addEventListener('click', toggle)
    39→    span.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
    40→      if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') {
    41→        e.preventDefault()
    42→        toggle()
    43→      }
    44→    })
    45→
    46→    return span
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    50→    return false
    51→  }
    52→
    53→  static importJSON(_serializedNode: SerializedElementNode): SpoilerNode {
    54→    return $createSpoilerNode()
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode {
    58→    return {
    59→      ...super.exportJSON(),
    60→      type: 'spoiler',
    61→      version: 1,
    62→    }
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  canInsertTextBefore(): boolean {
    66→    return true
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  canInsertTextAfter(): boolean {
    70→    return true
    71→  }
    72→
    73→  isInline(): boolean {
    74→    return true
    75→  }
    76→}
    77→
    78→export function $createSpoilerNode(): SpoilerNode {
    79→  return new SpoilerNode()
    80→}
    81→
    82→export function $isSpoilerNode(
    83→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
    84→): node is SpoilerNode {
    85→  return node instanceof SpoilerNode
    86→}
    87→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalNode,

     5→  SerializedElementNode,
     6→  Spread,
     7→} from 'lexical'
     8→import { $insertNodes, ElementNode } from 'lexical'
     9→import { ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react'
    10→import { createElement } from 'react'
    11→
    12→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '../types/slash-menu'
    13→import { createLucideSvg } from '../utils/lucide-dom'
    14→
    15→type IconNode = [string, Record<string, string>][]
    16→
    17→const ChevronDownIconNode: IconNode = [['path', { d: 'M6 8L10 12L14 8' }]]
    18→
    19→export type SerializedDetailsNode = Spread<
    20→  {
    21→    summary: string
    22→    open: boolean
    23→  },
    24→  SerializedElementNode
    25→>
    26→
    27→export class DetailsNode extends ElementNode {
    28→  __summary: string
    29→  __open: boolean
    30→
    31→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    32→    {
    33→      title: 'Details',
    34→      icon: createElement(ChevronRight, { size: 20 }),
    35→      description: 'Collapsible content block',
    36→      keywords: ['details', 'toggle', 'collapse', 'accordion'],
    37→      section: 'ADVANCED',
    38→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    39→        editor.update(() => {
    40→          $insertNodes([$createDetailsNode('Details')])
    41→        })
    42→      },
    43→    },
    44→  ]
    45→
    46→  static getType(): string {
    47→    return 'details'
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  static clone(node: DetailsNode): DetailsNode {
    51→    return new DetailsNode(node.__summary, node.__open, node.__key)
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  constructor(summary: string, open = false, key?: NodeKey) {
    55→    super(key)
    56→    this.__summary = summary
    57→    this.__open = open
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    61→    const details = document.createElement('details')
    62→    details.className = 'rich-details'
    63→    if (this.__open) {
    64→      details.open = true
    65→    }
    66→
    67→    const summary = document.createElement('summary')
    68→    summary.className = 'rich-details-summary'
    69→
    70→    const label = document.createElement('span')
    71→    label.className = 'rich-details-summary-text'
    72→    label.textContent = this.__summary
    73→    summary.append(label)
    74→
    75→    const chevron = document.createElement('span')
    76→    chevron.className = 'rich-details-chevron'
    77→    chevron.append(
    78→      createLucideSvg(ChevronDownIconNode, {
    79→        width: '20',
    80→        height: '20',
    81→        viewBox: '0 0 20 20',
    82→        'stroke-width': '1.5',
    83→      }),
    84→    )
    85→    summary.append(chevron)
    86→
    87→    const content = document.createElement('div')
    88→    content.className = 'rich-details-content'
    89→    details.append(summary, content)
    90→
    91→    return details
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  updateDOM(prevNode: DetailsNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
    95→    const details = dom as HTMLDetailsElement
    96→    if (prevNode.__open !== this.__open) {
    97→      details.open = this.__open
    98→    }
    99→    if (prevNode.__summary !== this.__summary) {
   100→      const label = dom.querySelector('.rich-details-summary-text')
   101→      if (label) {
   102→        label.textContent = this.__summary
   103→      }
   104→    }
   105→    return false
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedDetailsNode): DetailsNode {
   109→    return $createDetailsNode(serializedNode.summary, serializedNode.open)
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  exportJSON(): SerializedDetailsNode {
   113→    return {
   114→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   115→      type: 'details',
   116→      summary: this.__summary,
   117→      open: this.__open,
   118→      version: 1,
   119→    }
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  getSummary(): string {
   123→    return this.getLatest().__summary
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  setSummary(summary: string): void {
   127→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   128→    writable.__summary = summary
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  getOpen(): boolean {
   132→    return this.getLatest().__open
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  setOpen(open: boolean): void {
   136→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   137→    writable.__open = open
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  toggleOpen(): void {
   141→    this.setOpen(!this.getOpen())
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  getDOMSlot(element: HTMLElement) {
   145→    const content = element.querySelector(
   146→      '.rich-details-content',
   147→    ) as HTMLElement
   148→    return super.getDOMSlot(element).withElement(content)
   149→  }
   150→
   151→  isInline(): boolean {
   152→    return false
   153→  }
   154→}
   155→
   156→export function $createDetailsNode(summary: string, open = false): DetailsNode {
   157→  return new DetailsNode(summary, open)
   158→}
   159→
   160→export function $isDetailsNode(
   161→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   162→): node is DetailsNode {
   163→  return node instanceof DetailsNode
   164→}
   165→

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a clear understanding of all 6 issues. Let me also check the rich-style-token package since the `PortalThemeProvider` uses it, and the variant classes are composed from `richContent`, `articleTheme`/etc, and `articleBase`.

The key insight:
- `articleVariant` = `style([richContent, articleTheme, articleBase])` — this is a composed vanilla-extract class that includes THREE generated class names
- All `.rich-*` styles are scoped under `${richContent}` (from shared.css.ts) and `${articleBase}` (from article.css.ts)


- The SSR renderer needs to apply this same composed class to its root element for styles to work, but since it's in a separate package with zero Lexical dependency, I need to figure out whether the variant classes are exported from `@haklex/rich-style-token` instead of `@haklex/rich-editor`.
</details></think>

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report:

## Exploration Summary: `@haklex/rich-style-token` Package

### 1. Package Overview

**Location**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-style-token`

`@haklex/rich-style-token` is a **standalone, zero-dependency design token library** using Vanilla Extract for CSS-in-TS. It provides:
- CSS variable contracts via `vars`
- Theme color/layout presets (light/dark variants)
- Runtime theme overwriting utilities
- Portal theme context for nested components

### 2. Exports & API

**Main exports** (from `src/index.ts`):

```ts
// CSS variable contract (Vanilla Extract)
export { vars } from './vars.css'

// Runtime theme overwriting
export { createThemeStyle } from './create-theme-style'
export type { ThemeTokens } from './create-theme-style'

// Theme context for portals/iframes
export { PortalThemeProvider, PortalThemeWrapper, usePortalTheme } from './portal-theme'

// Preset colors & layouts
export {
  articleLayout, commentLayout, noteLayout,
  fonts,
  darkColors, lightArticleColors, lightCommentColors
} from './themes'
```

### 3. Core Structure

#### A. **vars.css.ts** - CSS Variable Contract

Creates a global theme contract via `createGlobalThemeContract()`:

```ts
vars = {
  color: { text, textSecondary, bg, bgSecondary, bgTertiary, border, 
           accent, accentLight, link, codeText, codeBg, quoteBorder, 
           quoteBg, alertInfo/Warning/Tip/Caution/Important },
  spacing: { xs, sm, md, lg, xl },
  typography: { fontFamily, fontFamilySans, fontFamilySerif, fontMono,
                fontSize2xs/Xs/Sm/Md/Lg/Base/Small, lineHeight, lineHeightTight },
  borderRadius: { sm, md, lg },
  layout: { maxWidth }
}
```

**Maps to CSS variables**: `--rc-text`, `--rc-space-md`, `--rc-font-size-base`, etc.

#### B. **themes.ts** - Preset Color/Layout Values

Three layout variants (article, note, comment) with shared colors:

```ts
// Font families (system/serif/mono)
fonts: { fontFamilySans, fontFamilySerif, fontMono }

// Color sets
lightArticleColors    // { text: '#000', accent: '#2563eb', ... }
lightCommentColors    // extends lightArticleColors with comment-specific tweaks
darkColors            // { text: '#fff', accent: '#60a5fa', ... }

// Layout definitions
articleLayout    // maxWidth: 700px, shared spacing/typography, sans fonts
noteLayout       // maxWidth: 700px, serif fonts, 1.8 line-height
commentLayout    // no max-width, smaller spacing/font (14px), sans fonts
```

#### C. **create-theme-style.ts** - Runtime Override Utility

Type-safe function to generate inline styles for CSS variable overrides:

```ts
const style = createThemeStyle({
  color: { accent: '#0ea5e9', text: '#0c4a6e' },
  spacing: { md: '20px' }
})
// Returns: { '--rc-accent': '#0ea5e9', '--rc-text': '#0c4a6e', '--rc-space-md': '20px' }

// Usage:
<div style={style}><RichEditor /></div>  // CSS vars cascade to children
```

#### D. **portal-theme.tsx** - Portal/iframe Theme Context

Provides theme classname to portaled content (e.g., popovers, dialogs in iframes):

```ts
<PortalThemeProvider className="articleVariant">
  <Portal>
    <PortalThemeWrapper>
      {/* Gets className applied via display:contents div */}
    </PortalThemeWrapper>
  </Portal>
</PortalThemeProvider>
```

### 4. Dependencies

**No production dependencies**—only React peer:
- `react: >=19` (peer)
- `@vanilla-extract/css` (build-time only)

### 5. Relationship to Variant Classes

**Variant classes are NOT exported from `rich-style-token`**—they live in `@haklex/rich-editor`:

| Class | Source | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `articleVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/article.css.ts` | Article display (16px, sans, 700px) |
| `darkArticleVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/article.css.ts` | Article dark mode |
| `noteVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/note.css.ts` | Note display (16px, serif, 700px) |
| `darkNoteVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/note.css.ts` | Note dark mode |
| `commentVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/comment.css.ts` | Comment display (14px, no max-width) |
| `darkCommentVariant` | `rich-editor/src/styles/comment.css.ts` | Comment dark mode |
| `richContent` | `rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts` | Base content styles (fonts, line-height, word-wrap) |

**How they're composed** (example from `article.css.ts`):
```ts
const articleBase = style({ maxWidth: vars.layout.maxWidth, fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeBase, ... })
export const articleVariant = style([richContent, articleTheme, articleBase])
// articleVariant = richContent + (theme CSS class) + article-specific spacing/heading sizes
```

**Theme classes** (from `rich-editor/src/styles/vars.css.ts`):
```ts
export const articleTheme = createTheme(vars, {
  color: lightArticleColors,
  ...articleLayout
})
// Generates a CSS class with CSS variable assignments
```

### 6. Can `rich-style-token` Be Used Independently?

**YES, absolutely.** The package is completely independent:

✅ **Zero Lexical dependencies** — Only Vanilla Extract (build-time)
✅ **Standalone usage possible** — Import `vars` + `createThemeStyle` anywhere
✅ **No editor bundle bloat** — Minimal footprint

```ts
// Standalone usage (no rich-editor needed)
import { vars, createThemeStyle } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'

// Create theme inline styles
const myTheme = createThemeStyle({
  color: { accent: '#ff0000', text: '#222' },
  spacing: { md: '20px' }
})

// Use vars for vanilla-extract CSS
import { style } from '@vanilla-extract/css'
export const myButton = style({
  color: vars.color.text,
  padding: vars.spacing.md,
  borderRadius: vars.borderRadius.md
})
```

### 7. Getting Variant CSS Classes WITHOUT Lexical

**Option A: Use `rich-editor` entry point** (includes Lexical as peer dep)
```ts
import { articleVariant, darkArticleVariant } from '@haklex/rich-editor/styles'
// This WILL require Lexical peers to be installed
```

**Option B: Extract variant logic to new package** (recommended if you need styles without editor)
```ts
// Hypothetical @haklex/rich-styles package:
// - Depends: @haklex/rich-style-token, @vanilla-extract/css
// - No Lexical dependency
// - Exports: articleVariant, noteVariant, commentVariant, etc.
// - Would re-create the style composition from rich-editor/src/styles/*.css.ts
```

**Option C: Copy variant styles locally**
```ts
// Copy src/styles/*.css.ts from rich-editor into your project
// Refactor imports to use @haklex/rich-style-token's vars
// No Lexical dependency in your copied version
```

### 8. Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Independence** | Fully independent; zero Lexical deps |
| **Build** | Vanilla Extract → zero-runtime CSS |
| **Exports** | `vars`, `createThemeStyle`, preset colors/layouts, portal theme |
| **Variant Classes** | NOT in this package; live in `@haklex/rich-editor` |
| **Dependency Graph** | `rich-editor` → `rich-style-token` (one-way) |
| **Bundle Impact** | Minimal (token definitions + vanilla-extract output) |
| **Peer Deps** | React >=19 only |
| **Use Cases** | Design tokens, theme customization, any Vanilla Extract project |

### Files Reference

- **Package**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-style-token/`
- **Tokens**: `src/vars.css.ts` (19 color vars, 5 spacing, 12 typography, 3 radius, 1 layout)
- **Presets**: `src/themes.ts` (3 color sets, 3 layout configs)
- **Runtime API**: `src/create-theme-style.ts` (type-safe override function)
- **Portal**: `src/portal-theme.tsx` (context provider for nested themes)
- **Variants (external)**: `rich-editor/src/styles/` (article, note, comment + dark variants)

 `./packages/rich-style-token/README.md`

```text
     1→# @haklex/rich-style-token
     2→
     3→设计令牌和 CSS 变量，基于 Vanilla Extract 实现零运行时样式。
     4→
     5→## 安装
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→pnpm add @haklex/rich-style-token
     9→```
    10→
    11→## 使用
    12→
    13→```tsx
    14→import { vars } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
    15→import { style } from '@vanilla-extract/css'
    16→
    17→export const container = style({
    18→  color: vars.color.text,
    19→  padding: vars.spacing.md,
    20→  borderRadius: vars.borderRadius.md,
    21→})
    22→```
    23→
    24→## 运行时颜色覆写
    25→
    26→主题契约生成标准 CSS 自定义属性（`--rc-*`），使用方可在运行时覆写。
    27→
    28→### 方式一：`createThemeStyle`（类型安全）
    29→
    30→```tsx
    31→import { createThemeStyle } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
    32→
    33→const style = createThemeStyle({
    34→  color: {
    35→    accent: '#0ea5e9',
    36→    text: '#0c4a6e',
    37→    bg: '#f0f9ff',
    38→  },
    39→})
    40→
    41→// CSS 变量向下级联，所有子组件自动生效
    42→<div style={style}>
    43→  <RichEditor />
    44→</div>
    45→```
    46→
    47→`ThemeTokens` 类型自动从 `vars` 契约推导，支持 `color`、`spacing`、`typography`、`borderRadius` 四组的任意子集覆写。
    48→
    49→### 方式二：纯 CSS
    50→
    51→```css
    52→.my-theme {
    53→  --rc-accent: #0ea5e9;
    54→  --rc-text: #0c4a6e;
    55→  --rc-bg: #f0f9ff;
    56→}
    57→```
    58→
    59→### 可覆写的 CSS 变量
    60→
    61→| 分组              | 变量                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
    62→| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    63→| **Color**         | `--rc-text`, `--rc-text-secondary`, `--rc-bg`, `--rc-bg-secondary`, `--rc-border`, `--rc-accent`, `--rc-accent-light`, `--rc-link`, `--rc-code-text`, `--rc-code-bg`, `--rc-quote-border`, `--rc-quote-bg`, `--rc-alert-info`, `--rc-alert-warning`, `--rc-alert-tip`, `--rc-alert-caution`, `--rc-alert-important` |
    64→| **Spacing**       | `--rc-space-xs`, `--rc-space-sm`, `--rc-space-md`, `--rc-space-lg`, `--rc-space-xl`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
    65→| **Typography**    | `--rc-font-family`, `--rc-font-mono`, `--rc-font-size-2xs`, `--rc-font-size-xs`, `--rc-font-size-sm`, `--rc-font-size-md`, `--rc-font-size-lg`, `--rc-font-size-base`, `--rc-font-size-small`, `--rc-line-height`, `--rc-line-height-tight`                                                                         |
    66→| **Border Radius** | `--rc-radius-sm`, `--rc-radius-md`, `--rc-radius-lg`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
    67→
    68→## 导出
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→// CSS 变量契约
    72→export { vars } from './vars.css'
    73→
    74→// 运行时覆写工具
    75→export { createThemeStyle } from './create-theme-style'
    76→export type { ThemeTokens } from './create-theme-style'
    77→
    78→// 主题配置
    79→export { articleLayout, commentLayout, noteLayout } from './themes'
    80→export { fonts } from './themes'
    81→export { darkColors, lightArticleColors, lightCommentColors } from './themes'
    82→
    83→// Portal 主题
    84→export {
    85→  PortalThemeProvider,
    86→  PortalThemeWrapper,
    87→  usePortalTheme,
    88→} from './portal-theme'
    89→```
    90→
    91→## 变量结构
    92→
    93→### vars.color
    94→
    95→```ts
    96→vars.color.text // 主文本色
    97→vars.color.textSecondary // 次要文本
    98→vars.color.bg // 背景色
    99→vars.color.bgSecondary // 次要背景
   100→vars.color.border // 边框色

   103→vars.color.link // 链接色
   104→vars.color.codeText // 代码文本
   105→vars.color.codeBg // 代码背景
   106→vars.color.quoteBorder // 引用边框
   107→vars.color.quoteBg // 引用背景
   108→vars.color.alertInfo // 提示色

   111→vars.color.alertCaution // 注意色

   114→
   115→### vars.spacing

   120→vars.spacing.md // article: 16px, comment: 10px
   121→vars.spacing.lg // article: 24px, comment: 16px

   124→

   134→vars.typography.fontSizeLg // 大字号（1.25em）

   136→vars.typography.fontSizeSmall // 兼容小字号（px）
   137→vars.typography.lineHeight // 行高
   138→vars.typography.lineHeightTight // 紧行高
   139→```
   140→

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-style-token/src/create-theme-style.ts`

```text
     1→import type { CSSProperties } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { vars } from './vars.css'
     4→
     5→type VarsContract = typeof vars
     6→
     7→export type ThemeTokens = {
     8→  [K in keyof VarsContract]?: Partial<Record<keyof VarsContract[K], string>>
     9→}
    10→
    11→function extractVarName(cssVarRef: string): string {
    12→  const match = cssVarRef.match(/^var\((.+)\)$/)
    13→  return match ? match[1] : cssVarRef
    14→}
    15→
    16→export function createThemeStyle(tokens: ThemeTokens): CSSProperties {
    17→  const style: Record<string, string> = {}
    18→
    19→  for (const [group, values] of Object.entries(tokens)) {
    20→    if (!values) continue
    21→    const contractGroup = vars[group as keyof VarsContract] as Record<
    22→      string,
    23→      string
    24→    >
    25→    if (!contractGroup) continue
    26→    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(values)) {
    27→      if (value == null) continue
    28→      const cssVarRef = contractGroup[key]
    29→      if (cssVarRef) {
    30→        style[extractVarName(cssVarRef)] = value
    31→      }
    32→    }
    33→  }
    34→
    35→  return style as CSSProperties
    36→}
    37→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-style-token/src/portal-theme.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     2→import { createContext, use, useMemo } from 'react'
     3→
     4→type PortalThemeContextType = {
     5→  className: string
     6→}
     7→
     8→const PortalThemeContext = createContext<PortalThemeContextType>({
     9→  className: '',
    10→})
    11→
    12→export function PortalThemeProvider({
    13→  className,
    14→  children,
    15→}: {
    16→  className: string
    17→  children?: ReactNode
    18→}) {
    19→  return (
    20→    <PortalThemeContext.Provider
    21→      value={useMemo(() => ({ className }), [className])}
    22→    >
    23→      {children}
    24→    </PortalThemeContext.Provider>
    25→  )
    26→}
    27→
    28→export function usePortalTheme() {
    29→  return use(PortalThemeContext)
    30→}
    31→
    32→export function PortalThemeWrapper({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
    33→  const { className } = usePortalTheme()
    34→  if (!className) return children
    35→  return (
    36→    <div style={{ display: 'contents' }} className={className}>
    37→      {children}
    38→    </div>
    39→  )
    40→}
    41→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-25 09:42:08Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/vite.shared.ts`

```text
     1→import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import type { UserConfig } from 'vite'
     5→
     6→export interface SharedViteOptions {
     7→  entry?: string | Record<string, string>
     8→  vanillaExtract?: boolean
     9→  esbuild?: { jsx: string; jsxImportSource: string }
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BASE_EXTERNALS = ['react', 'react-dom', 'react/jsx-runtime']
    13→
    14→export async function createViteConfig(
    15→  options: SharedViteOptions = {},
    16→): Promise<UserConfig> {
    17→  const { entry = 'src/index.ts', vanillaExtract = true, esbuild } = options
    18→
    19→  const dts = (await import('vite-plugin-dts')).default
    20→  const plugins: UserConfig['plugins'] = []
    21→
    22→  if (vanillaExtract) {
    23→    const { vanillaExtractPlugin } =
    24→      await import('@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin')
    25→    plugins.push(vanillaExtractPlugin())
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  plugins.push(
    29→    dts({ entryRoot: 'src', outDir: 'dist', tsconfigPath: './tsconfig.json' }),
    30→  )
    31→
    32→  const cwd = process.cwd()
    33→  const isMap = typeof entry !== 'string'
    34→  const libEntry = isMap
    35→    ? Object.fromEntries(
    36→        Object.entries(entry).map(([k, v]) => [k, resolve(cwd, v)]),
    37→      )
    38→    : resolve(cwd, entry)
    39→
    40→  const fileName = isMap
    41→    ? (_: string, name: string) => `${name}.mjs`
    42→    : () => 'index.mjs'
    43→
    44→  const pkg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(resolve(cwd, 'package.json'), 'utf-8'))
    45→  const depNames = new Set([
    46→    ...BASE_EXTERNALS,
    47→    ...Object.keys(pkg.dependencies || {}),
    48→    ...Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies || {}),
    49→  ])
    50→
    51→  const rollupExternal = (id: string) => {
    52→    if (id[0] === '.' || id[0] === '/' || id[0] === '\0') return false
    53→    if (depNames.has(id)) return true
    54→    const slashIdx = id.indexOf('/')
    55→    if (slashIdx > 0) {
    56→      const pkgName =
    57→        id[0] === '@'
    58→          ? id.slice(0, id.indexOf('/', slashIdx + 1))
    59→          : id.slice(0, slashIdx)
    60→      return pkgName ? depNames.has(pkgName) : false
    61→    }
    62→    return false
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  return {
    66→    plugins,
    67→    ...(esbuild && { esbuild }),
    68→    build: {
    69→      lib: { entry: libEntry, formats: ['es'], fileName },
    70→      rollupOptions: {
    71→        external: rollupExternal,
    72→        output: { preserveModules: false },
    73→      },
    74→      cssCodeSplit: false,
    75→      minify: false,
    76→      cssMinify: true,
    77→      target: 'es2020',
    78→    },
    79→  }
    80→}
    81→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { globalStyle, style } from '@vanilla-extract/css'
     2→
     3→import { richContent } from './shared.css'
     4→import { articleTheme, darkArticleTheme, vars } from './vars.css'
     5→
     6→// Helper for em calculation (based on 16px base)
     7→const em = (target: number, context = 16) => `${target / context}em`
     8→const round = (num: number) => num.toFixed(7).replace(/(\.\d+?)0+$/, '$1')
     9→
    10→// Prose-style typography
    11→// Base font size: 16px
    12→
    13→const articleBase = style({
    14→  maxWidth: vars.layout.maxWidth,
    15→  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeBase,
    16→  lineHeight: round(28 / 16), // 1.75
    17→  color: vars.color.text,
    18→})
    19→
    20→export const articleVariant = style([richContent, articleTheme, articleBase])
    21→export const darkArticleVariant = style([
    22→  richContent,
    23→  darkArticleTheme,
    24→  articleBase,
    25→])
    26→
    27→// ─── Tailwind Prose LG Styles ───────────────────────────
    28→// Reference: https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss-typography/blob/master/src/styles.js
    29→
    30→globalStyle(`${articleBase} .rich-paragraph`, {
    31→  marginTop: em(24, 16),
    32→  marginBottom: em(24, 16),
    33→})
    34→
    35→globalStyle(`${articleBase} .rich-heading-h1`, {
    36→  color: vars.color.text,
    37→  fontWeight: 800,
    38→  fontSize: em(48, 16),
    39→  marginTop: '0',
    40→  marginBottom: em(40, 48), // 0.8333333em
    41→  lineHeight: 1,
    42→})
    43→
    44→globalStyle(`${articleBase} .rich-heading-h2`, {
    45→  color: vars.color.text,
    46→  fontWeight: 700,
    47→  fontSize: em(30, 16),
    48→  marginTop: em(56, 30), // 1.8666667em
    49→  marginBottom: em(32, 30), // 1.0666667em
    50→  lineHeight: round(40 / 30), // 1.3333333
```
</details></tool-use>

   597→
   598→// ─── Alert (unified across variants) ────────────────────
   599→globalStyle(`${richContent} .rich-alert`, {
   600→  margin: '2em 0',
   601→  padding: '0 1em',
   602→  backgroundColor: 'transparent',
   603→  border: 'none',
   604→  borderRadius: 0,
   605→})
   606→
   607→// ─── First-child reset ──────────────────────────────────
   608→// Editor mode adds a `.rich-editor__content-wrapper` between variant root and blocks.
   609→// Keep first/last child margin reset working for both renderer and editor trees.
   610→globalStyle(
   611→  `${richContent} > *:first-child, ${richContent} .rich-editor__content > *:first-child`,
   612→  {
   613→    marginTop: 0,
   614→  },
   615→)
   616→
   617→globalStyle(
   618→  `${richContent} > *:last-child, ${richContent} .rich-editor__content > *:last-child`,
   619→  {
   620→    marginBottom: 0,
   621→  },
   622→)
   623→
```
</details></tool-use>
