     1→import type { MermaidRendererProps } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import type { FC } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import * as css from './styles.css'
     5→import { useMermaidRender } from './useMermaidRender'
     6→
     7→export const MermaidRenderer: FC<MermaidRendererProps> = ({ content }) => {
     8→  const { loading, error, imgSrc, width, height } = useMermaidRender(content)
     9→
    10→  if (loading) {
    11→    return <div className={css.mermaidLoading}>Mermaid Loading</div>
    12→  }
    13→
    14→  if (!imgSrc) {
    15→    return <div className={css.mermaidError}>{error || 'Render failed'}</div>
    16→  }
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <div className={css.mermaidContainer} style={{ cursor: 'default' }}>
    20→      <img src={imgSrc} alt="Mermaid diagram" width={width} height={height} />
    21→    </div>
    22→  )
    23→}
    24→
    25→export default MermaidRenderer
    26→

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-ext-tldraw/src/TldrawStaticRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { presentDialog } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     3→import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     4→import { Maximize2, ScanSearch, X, ZoomIn, ZoomOut } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import { Component, type FC, type ReactNode } from 'react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import * as css from './styles.css'
     9→import { useTldrawData } from './useTldrawData'
    10→
    11→export interface TldrawStaticRendererProps {
    12→  snapshot: string
    13→}
    14→
    15→export const TldrawStaticRenderer: FC<TldrawStaticRendererProps> = ({
    16→  snapshot,
    17→}) => {
    18→  const theme = useColorScheme()
    19→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    20→  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)
    21→
    22→  useEffect(() => {
    23→    if (hasEnteredView) return
    24→    const target = containerRef.current
    25→    if (!target) return
    26→
    27→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    28→      (entries) => {
    29→        if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) {
    30→          setHasEnteredView(true)
    31→          observer.disconnect()
    32→        }
    33→      },
    34→      { rootMargin: '200px 0px', threshold: 0.1 },
    35→    )
    36→    observer.observe(target)
    37→    return () => observer.disconnect()
    38→  }, [hasEnteredView])
    39→
    40→  if (!hasEnteredView) {
    41→    return (
    42→      <div ref={containerRef} className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
    43→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
    44→      </div>
    45→    )
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  return (
    49→    <div ref={containerRef}>
    50→      <TldrawStaticCanvas snapshot={snapshot} theme={theme} />
    51→    </div>
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→// --- Fullscreen readonly content ---
    56→
    57→const TldrawExpandContent: FC<{
    58→  dismiss: () => void
    59→  TldrawComponent: FC<any>
    60→  data: Record<string, any>
    61→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    62→}> = ({ dismiss, TldrawComponent, data, theme }) => (
    63→  <>
    64→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogHeader}>
    65→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogTitle}>Whiteboard</span>
    66→      <span className={css.tldrawDialogMeta}>tldraw</span>
    67→      <button type="button" className={css.tldrawHeaderClose} onClick={dismiss}>
    68→        <X size={18} />
    69→      </button>
    70→    </div>
    71→    <div className={css.tldrawDialogCanvas}>
    72→      <TldrawComponent
    73→        snapshot={data}
    74→        hideUi
    75→        onMount={(editor: any) => {
    76→          editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
    77→          editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
    78→          editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
    79→          setTimeout(() => editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false }), 100)
    80→        }}
    81→      />
    82→    </div>
    83→  </>
    84→)
    85→
    86→// --- Main Canvas ---
    87→
    88→const TldrawStaticCanvas: FC<{
    89→  snapshot: string
    90→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    91→}> = ({ snapshot, theme }) => {
    92→  const {
    93→    snapshot: data,
    94→    loading: dataLoading,
    95→    error: dataError,
    96→  } = useTldrawData(snapshot)
    97→  const [TldrawComponent, setTldrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
    98→  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
    99→  const editorRef = useRef<any>(null)
   100→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   101→
   102→  useEffect(() => {
   103→    import('tldraw')
   104→      .then((mod) => {
   105→        setTldrawComponent(() => mod.Tldraw)
   106→        setLibLoading(false)
   107→      })
   108→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   109→  }, [])
   110→
   111→  useEffect(() => {
   112→    if (editorRef.current?.user?.updateUserPreferences) {
   113→      editorRef.current.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   114→    }
   115→  }, [theme])
   116→
   117→  const handleMount = useCallback(
   118→    (editor: any) => {
   119→      editorRef.current = editor
   120→      editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })
   121→      editor.setCurrentTool('hand')
   122→      editor.user.updateUserPreferences({ colorScheme: theme })
   123→      setTimeout(() => {
   124→        editor.zoomToFit({ animate: false })
   125→      }, 100)
   126→    },
   127→    [theme],
   128→  )
   129→
   130→  const handleExpand = useCallback(() => {
   131→    if (!TldrawComponent || !data) return
   132→    presentDialog({
   133→      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
   134→        <TldrawExpandContent
   135→          dismiss={dismiss}
   136→          TldrawComponent={TldrawComponent}
   137→          data={data}
   138→          theme={theme}
   139→        />
   140→      ),
   141→      className: css.tldrawFullscreenPopup,
   142→      portalClassName,
   143→      showCloseButton: false,
   144→      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
   145→    })
   146→  }, [TldrawComponent, data, theme, portalClassName])
   147→
   148→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   149→
   150→  if (loading) {
   151→    return (
   152→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   153→        <div className={css.tldrawLoading}>Loading tldraw...</div>
   154→      </div>
   155→    )
   156→  }
   157→
   158→  if (dataError || !data) {
   159→    return (
   160→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   161→        <div className={css.tldrawError}>{dataError || 'No data'}</div>
   162→      </div>
   163→    )
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  if (!TldrawComponent) {
   167→    return (
   168→      <div className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}>
   169→        <div className={css.tldrawError}>Failed to load tldraw</div>
   170→      </div>
   171→    )
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  return (
   175→    <div
   176→      className={css.tldrawStaticContainer}
   177→      data-theme={theme}
   178→      data-color-scheme={theme}
   179→    >
   180→      <TldrawErrorBoundary
   181→        fallback={
   182→          <div className={css.tldrawError}>Failed to render tldraw</div>
   183→        }
   184→      >
   185→        <TldrawComponent snapshot={data} hideUi onMount={handleMount} />
   186→      </TldrawErrorBoundary>
   187→
   188→      <div className={css.tldrawActionGroup}>
   189→        <button
   190→          type="button"
   191→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   192→          title="Zoom In"
   193→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomIn()}
   194→        >
   195→          <ZoomIn size={20} />
   196→        </button>
   197→        <button
   198→          type="button"
   199→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   200→          title="Zoom Out"
   201→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomOut()}
   202→        >
   203→          <ZoomOut size={20} />
   204→        </button>
   205→        <button
   206→          type="button"
   207→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   208→          title="Fit to Content"
   209→          onClick={() => editorRef.current?.zoomToFit()}
   210→        >
   211→          <ScanSearch size={20} />
   212→        </button>
   213→        <button
   214→          type="button"
   215→          className={css.tldrawActionButton}
   216→          title="Expand"
   217→          onClick={handleExpand}
   218→        >
   219→          <Maximize2 size={20} />
   220→        </button>
   221→      </div>
   222→    </div>
   223→  )
   224→}
   225→
   226→class TldrawErrorBoundary extends Component<
   227→  { fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode },
   228→  { hasError: boolean }
   229→> {
   230→  state = { hasError: false }
   231→  static getDerivedStateFromError() {
   232→    return { hasError: true }
   233→  }
   234→  render() {
   235→    return this.state.hasError ? this.props.fallback : this.props.children
   236→  }
   237→}
   238→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./packages/rich-renderer-image/src/ImageRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import 'react-photo-view/dist/react-photo-view.css'
     2→
     3→import { decodeThumbHash, type ImageRendererProps } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     4→import type { ComponentType, CSSProperties } from 'react'
     5→import { useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     6→import { PhotoProvider, PhotoView } from 'react-photo-view'
     7→
     8→import * as styles from './styles.css'
     9→
    10→type ImageLoadState = 'loading' | 'loaded' | 'error'
    11→
    12→const frameStateSemanticClass: Record<ImageLoadState, string> = {
    13→  loading: styles.semanticClassNames.frameLoading,
    14→  loaded: styles.semanticClassNames.frameLoaded,
    15→  error: styles.semanticClassNames.frameError,
    16→}
    17→
    18→function getCaptionText(altText: string, caption?: string): string | undefined {
    19→  if (caption) return caption
    20→  if (!altText) return undefined
    21→  if (altText.startsWith('!') || altText.startsWith('¡')) {
    22→    return altText.slice(1)
    23→  }
    24→  return undefined
    25→}
    26→
    27→export const ImageRenderer: ComponentType<ImageRendererProps> = ({
    28→  src,
    29→  altText,
    30→  width,
    31→  height,
    32→  caption,
    33→  thumbhash,
    34→  accent,
    35→}) => {
    36→  const [state, setState] = useState<ImageLoadState>('loading')
    37→
    38→  const captionText = useMemo(
    39→    () => getCaptionText(altText, caption),
    40→    [altText, caption],
    41→  )
    42→
    43→  const placeholderUrl = useMemo(
    44→    () => (thumbhash ? decodeThumbHash(thumbhash) : undefined),
    45→    [thumbhash],
    46→  )
    47→
    48→  if (!src) return null
    49→
    50→  const frameStyle: CSSProperties = {
    51→    backgroundColor: !placeholderUrl ? accent || '#f5f5f5' : undefined,
    52→    backgroundImage:
    53→      placeholderUrl && state !== 'loaded'
    54→        ? `url(${placeholderUrl})`
    55→        : undefined,
    56→    backgroundSize: 'cover',
    57→    width: width ? Math.min(width, 1200) : undefined,
    58→    maxWidth: '100%',
    59→    ...(width && height ? { aspectRatio: `${width} / ${height}` } : {}),
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  return (
    63→    <figure className={`${styles.root} ${styles.semanticClassNames.root}`}>
    64→      <PhotoProvider photoClosable>
    65→        <PhotoView src={src}>
    66→          <div
    67→            className={`${styles.frame} ${styles.semanticClassNames.frame} ${styles.imageState[state]} ${frameStateSemanticClass[state]}`.trim()}
    68→            style={frameStyle}
    69→            role="button"
    70→            tabIndex={0}
    71→            aria-label={`Open image: ${altText || 'image'}`}
    72→            onKeyDown={(e) => {
    73→              if (e.key !== 'Enter' && e.key !== ' ') return
    74→              e.preventDefault()
    75→              e.currentTarget.click()
    76→            }}
    77→          >
    78→            <img
    79→              src={src}
    80→              alt={altText}
    81→              width={width}
    82→              height={height}
    83→              loading="lazy"
    84→              className={`${styles.image} ${styles.semanticClassNames.image}`}
    85→              onLoad={() => setState('loaded')}
    86→              onError={() => setState('error')}
    87→            />
    88→
    89→            {state === 'loading' && (
    90→              <span
    91→                className={`${styles.loader} ${styles.semanticClassNames.loader}`}
    92→              />
    93→            )}
    94→
    95→            {state === 'error' && (
    96→              <span
    97→                className={`${styles.errorBadge} ${styles.semanticClassNames.errorBadge}`}
    98→              >
    99→                Image failed to load
   100→              </span>
   101→            )}
   102→          </div>
   103→        </PhotoView>
   104→      </PhotoProvider>
   105→
   106→      {captionText && (
   107→        <figcaption
   108→          className={`${styles.caption} ${styles.semanticClassNames.caption}`}
   109→        >
   110→          {captionText}
   111→        </figcaption>
   112→      )}
   113→    </figure>
   114→  )
   115→}
   116→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedEditorState,
     7→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     8→  Spread,
     9→} from 'lexical'
    10→import { $getRoot, $insertNodes, createEditor, DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import {
    12→  Info,
    13→  Lightbulb,
    14→  TriangleAlert as TriangleAlertIcon,
    15→} from 'lucide-react'
    16→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    17→import { createElement } from 'react'
    18→
    19→import { AlertReadOnlyDecorator } from '../components/renderers/AlertReadOnlyDecorator'
    20→import { editorTheme } from '../styles/theme'
    21→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '../types/slash-menu'
    22→import { NESTED_EDITOR_NODES } from './shared'
    23→
    24→export type AlertType = 'note' | 'tip' | 'important' | 'warning' | 'caution'
    25→
    26→export const ALERT_TYPES: AlertType[] = [
    27→  'note',
    28→  'tip',
    29→  'important',
    30→  'warning',
    31→  'caution',
    32→]
    33→
    34→export const ALERT_LABELS: Record<AlertType, string> = {
    35→  note: 'Note',
    36→  tip: 'Tip',
    37→  important: 'Important',
    38→  warning: 'Warning',
    39→  caution: 'Caution',
    40→}
    41→
    42→function createContentEditor(): LexicalEditor {
    43→  return createEditor({
    44→    namespace: 'AlertContent',
    45→    nodes: NESTED_EDITOR_NODES,
    46→    theme: editorTheme,
    47→    onError: (error: Error) => {
    48→      console.error('[AlertContent]', error)
    49→    },
    50→  })
    51→}
    52→
    53→export type SerializedAlertQuoteNode = Spread<
    54→  {
    55→    alertType: AlertType
    56→    content: SerializedEditorState
    57→  },
    58→  SerializedLexicalNode
    59→>
    60→
    61→export class AlertQuoteNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    62→  __alertType: AlertType
    63→  __contentEditor: LexicalEditor
    64→
    65→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    66→    {
    67→      title: 'Callout',
    68→      icon: createElement(Info, { size: 20 }),
    69→      description: 'Info callout block',
    70→      keywords: ['alert', 'note', 'info', 'callout'],
    71→      section: 'ADVANCED',
    72→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    73→        editor.update(() => {
    74→          $insertNodes([$createAlertQuoteNode('note')])
    75→        })
    76→      },
    77→    },
    78→    {
    79→      title: 'Tip',
    80→      icon: createElement(Lightbulb, { size: 20 }),
    81→      description: 'Highlight a useful tip',
    82→      keywords: ['alert', 'tip', 'hint'],
    83→      section: 'ADVANCED',
    84→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    85→        editor.update(() => {
    86→          $insertNodes([$createAlertQuoteNode('tip')])
    87→        })
    88→      },
    89→    },
    90→    {
    91→      title: 'Warning',
    92→      icon: createElement(TriangleAlertIcon, { size: 20 }),
    93→      description: 'Warn about something',
    94→      keywords: ['alert', 'warning', 'caution'],
    95→      section: 'ADVANCED',
    96→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    97→        editor.update(() => {
    98→          $insertNodes([$createAlertQuoteNode('warning')])
    99→        })
   100→      },
   101→    },
   102→  ]
   103→
   104→  static getType(): string {
   105→    return 'alert-quote'
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  static clone(node: AlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
   109→    return new AlertQuoteNode(
   110→      node.__alertType,
   111→      node.__contentEditor,
   112→      node.__key,
   113→    )
   114→  }
   115→
   116→  constructor(
   117→    alertType: AlertType,
   118→    contentEditor?: LexicalEditor,
   119→    key?: NodeKey,
   120→  ) {
   121→    super(key)
   122→    this.__alertType = alertType
   123→    this.__contentEditor = contentEditor || createContentEditor()
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   127→    const div = document.createElement('div')
   128→    div.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
   129→    return div
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  updateDOM(prevNode: AlertQuoteNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
   133→    if (prevNode.__alertType !== this.__alertType) {
   134→      dom.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
   135→    }
   136→    return false
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  isInline(): boolean {
   140→    return false
   141→  }
   142→
   143→  getAlertType(): AlertType {
   144→    return this.__alertType
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  setAlertType(alertType: AlertType): void {
   148→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   149→    writable.__alertType = alertType
   150→  }
   151→
   152→  getContentEditor(): LexicalEditor {
   153→    return this.__contentEditor
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  getTextContent(): string {
   157→    return this.__contentEditor.getEditorState().read(() => {
   158→      return $getRoot().getTextContent()
   159→    })
   160→  }
   161→
   162→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedAlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
   163→    const node = new AlertQuoteNode(serializedNode.alertType)
   164→    if (serializedNode.content) {
   165→      const editorState = node.__contentEditor.parseEditorState(
   166→        serializedNode.content,
   167→      )
   168→      node.__contentEditor.setEditorState(editorState)
   169→    }
   170→    return node
   171→  }
   172→
   173→  exportJSON(): SerializedAlertQuoteNode {
   174→    return {
   175→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   176→      type: 'alert-quote',
   177→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   178→      content: this.__contentEditor.getEditorState().toJSON(),
   179→      version: 1,
   180→    }
   181→  }
   182→
   183→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   184→    return createElement(AlertReadOnlyDecorator, {
   185→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   186→      contentEditor: this.__contentEditor,
   187→    })
   188→  }
   189→}
   190→
   191→export function $createAlertQuoteNode(alertType: AlertType): AlertQuoteNode {
   192→  return new AlertQuoteNode(alertType)
   193→}
   194→
   195→export function $isAlertQuoteNode(
   196→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   197→): node is AlertQuoteNode {
   198→  return node instanceof AlertQuoteNode
   199→}
   200→
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/BannerNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedEditorState,
     7→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     8→  Spread,
     9→} from 'lexical'
    10→import { $getRoot, $insertNodes, createEditor, DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import { Flag } from 'lucide-react'
    12→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    13→import { createElement } from 'react'
    14→
    15→import { BannerReadOnlyDecorator } from '../components/renderers/BannerReadOnlyDecorator'
    16→import { editorTheme } from '../styles/theme'
    17→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '../types/slash-menu'
    18→import { NESTED_EDITOR_NODES } from './shared'
    19→
    20→export type BannerType = 'note' | 'tip' | 'important' | 'warning' | 'caution'
    21→
    22→const LEGACY_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, BannerType> = {
    23→  info: 'note',
    24→  success: 'tip',
    25→  error: 'caution',
    26→}
    27→
    28→export function normalizeBannerType(type: string): BannerType {
    29→  if (type in LEGACY_TYPE_MAP) return LEGACY_TYPE_MAP[type]
    30→  return (type as BannerType) || 'note'
    31→}
    32→
    33→export const BANNER_TYPES: BannerType[] = [
    34→  'note',
    35→  'tip',
    36→  'important',
    37→  'warning',
    38→  'caution',
    39→]
    40→
    41→export const BANNER_LABELS: Record<BannerType, string> = {
    42→  note: 'Note',
    43→  tip: 'Tip',
    44→  important: 'Important',
    45→  warning: 'Warning',
    46→  caution: 'Caution',
    47→}
    48→
    49→function createContentEditor(): LexicalEditor {
    50→  return createEditor({
    51→    namespace: 'BannerContent',
    52→    nodes: NESTED_EDITOR_NODES,
    53→    theme: editorTheme,
    54→    onError: (error: Error) => {
    55→      console.error('[BannerContent]', error)
    56→    },
    57→  })
    58→}
    59→
    60→export type SerializedBannerNode = Spread<
    61→  {
    62→    bannerType: BannerType
    63→    content: SerializedEditorState
    64→  },
    65→  SerializedLexicalNode
    66→>
    67→
    68→interface LegacySerializedBannerNode extends SerializedBannerNode {
    69→  children?: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    70→}
    71→
    72→export class BannerNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    73→  __bannerType: BannerType
    74→  __contentEditor: LexicalEditor
    75→
    76→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    77→    {
    78→      title: 'Banner',
    79→      icon: createElement(Flag, { size: 20 }),
    80→      description: 'Highlighted banner block',
    81→      keywords: ['banner', 'notice', 'announcement'],
    82→      section: 'ADVANCED',
    83→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    84→        editor.update(() => {
    85→          $insertNodes([$createBannerNode('note')])
    86→        })
    87→      },
    88→    },
    89→  ]
    90→
    91→  static getType(): string {
    92→    return 'banner'
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  static clone(node: BannerNode): BannerNode {
    96→    return new BannerNode(node.__bannerType, node.__contentEditor, node.__key)
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  constructor(
   100→    bannerType: BannerType,
   101→    contentEditor?: LexicalEditor,
   102→    key?: NodeKey,
   103→  ) {
   104→    super(key)
   105→    this.__bannerType = bannerType
   106→    this.__contentEditor = contentEditor || createContentEditor()
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   110→    const div = document.createElement('div')
   111→    div.className = `rich-banner rich-banner-${this.__bannerType}`
   112→    return div
   113→  }
   114→
   115→  updateDOM(prevNode: BannerNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
   116→    if (prevNode.__bannerType !== this.__bannerType) {
   117→      dom.className = `rich-banner rich-banner-${this.__bannerType}`
   118→    }
   119→    return false
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  isInline(): boolean {
   123→    return false
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  getBannerType(): BannerType {
   127→    return this.__bannerType
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  setBannerType(bannerType: BannerType): void {
   131→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   132→    writable.__bannerType = bannerType
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  getContentEditor(): LexicalEditor {
   136→    return this.__contentEditor
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  getTextContent(): string {
   140→    return this.__contentEditor.getEditorState().read(() => {
   141→      return $getRoot().getTextContent()
   142→    })
   143→  }
   144→
   145→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedBannerNode): BannerNode {
   146→    const legacy = serializedNode as LegacySerializedBannerNode
   147→    const bannerType = normalizeBannerType(serializedNode.bannerType)
   148→    const node = new BannerNode(bannerType)
   149→
   150→    if (serializedNode.content) {
   151→      const editorState = node.__contentEditor.parseEditorState(
   152→        serializedNode.content,
   153→      )
   154→      node.__contentEditor.setEditorState(editorState)
   155→    } else if (legacy.children) {
   156→      // Legacy ElementNode format: wrap children into editor state
   157→      const content = {
   158→        root: {
   159→          children: legacy.children,
   160→          direction: null,
   161→          format: '',
   162→          indent: 0,
   163→          type: 'root',
   164→          version: 1,
   165→        },
   166→      } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
   167→      const editorState = node.__contentEditor.parseEditorState(content)
   168→      node.__contentEditor.setEditorState(editorState)
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    return node
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  exportJSON(): SerializedBannerNode {
   175→    return {
   176→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   177→      type: 'banner',
   178→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   179→      content: this.__contentEditor.getEditorState().toJSON(),
   180→      version: 1,
   181→    }
   182→  }
   183→
   184→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   185→    return createElement(BannerReadOnlyDecorator, {
   186→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   187→      contentEditor: this.__contentEditor,
   188→    })
   189→  }
   190→}
   191→
   192→export function $createBannerNode(bannerType: BannerType): BannerNode {
   193→  return new BannerNode(bannerType)
   194→}
   195→
   196→export function $isBannerNode(
   197→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   198→): node is BannerNode {
   199→  return node instanceof BannerNode
   200→}
   201→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="Task"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Task**</summary>
Explore headless editor creation

```text
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile a comprehensive research report.

---

## Research Report: Using `@lexical/headless` in the Shiroi Project

### 1. **Discovery: `@lexical/headless` Package Exists Separately**

The `@lexical/headless` is a **separate official npm package** from Meta/Lexical, distinct from the main `lexical` package. Key documentation sources:
- Official docs: https://lexical.dev/docs/packages/lexical-headless
- NPM package: https://lexical.dev/docs/packages/lexical-headless

**Current Status in Shiroi**: The package is **NOT currently installed**. The project only has:
- `lexical@0.40.0` (core)
- Custom `@haklex/rich-headless` package that provides node registry and transformers

### 2. **API: `createHeadlessEditor` Function**

**Import**:
```typescript
import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
```

**Configuration** (follows standard `CreateEditorArgs`):
```typescript
const editor = createHeadlessEditor({
  nodes: allHeadlessNodes,        // Required: Klass<LexicalNode>[]
  namespace?: string,              // Optional: editor namespace
  onError?: (error: Error) => void,// Optional: error handler
  editable?: boolean,              // Optional: default false for headless
  theme?: EditorThemeClasses,      // Optional: theme config
})
```

The function creates a `LexicalEditor` instance with `_headless: true` flag, disabling DOM-dependent operations.

### 3. **Parsing JSON to Nodes: `editor.parseEditorState()`**

**Function signature** (from `LexicalEditor` types):
```typescript
parseEditorState(
  maybeStringifiedEditorState: string | SerializedEditorState,
  updateFn?: () => void
): EditorState
```

**SerializedEditorState Type**:
```typescript
interface SerializedEditorState<T extends SerializedLexicalNode = SerializedLexicalNode> {
  root: SerializedRootNode<T>
}
```

**Real-world usage from `GridContainerNode.ts`** (lines 182–186):
```typescript
const editor = createCellEditor()
const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(cellState)  // cellState: SerializedEditorState
editor.setEditorState(editorState)
```

### 4. **Traversing Node Tree: `editorState.read()`**

**EditorState.read() API**:
```typescript
class EditorState {
  read<V>(callbackFn: () => V, options?: EditorStateReadOptions): V
}
```

**Key behaviors**:
- Executes callback within editor context (allows calling `$`-prefixed APIs)
- Works in headless mode (no DOM needed)
- Returns the callback's result
- All node access must occur inside `read()` context

**Real-world usage from `FootnotePlugin.tsx`** (lines 22–42):
```typescript
editorState.read(() => {
  const footnoteNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteNode)
  const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
  // ... iterate and read nodes
})
```

**Another example from `GridContainerNode.ts`** (lines 156–158):
```typescript
const isEmpty = editor.getEditorState().read(() => {
  return $getRoot().getTextContentSize() === 0
})
```

### 5. **DecoratorNode and `decorate()` Method**

**DecoratorNode interface** (from `LexicalDecoratorNode.d.ts`):
```typescript
export class DecoratorNode<T> extends LexicalNode {
  decorate(editor: LexicalEditor, config: EditorConfig): null | T
  isIsolated(): boolean
  isInline(): boolean
  isKeyboardSelectable(): boolean
}
```

**Headless-compatible nodes in `@haklex/rich-headless`** (lines 140–195 of `index.ts`):
- All custom decorator nodes have `decorate()` implementations that return `null` (since no React rendering needed in headless mode)
- Example (lines 181–189):
```typescript
class Node extends DecoratorNode<null> {
  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    return stubDOM()  // Returns dummy HTMLElement, never called in headless
  }
  updateDOM(): boolean {
    return false
  }
  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): null {
    return null  // No decorator rendering in headless
  }
}
```

### 6. **Can This Work Without Browser APIs?**

✅ **YES, with qualifications**:

| Component | Headless Compatible | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|-------|
| `createHeadlessEditor()` | ✅ Yes | Creates editor without root element |
| `editor.parseEditorState(json)` | ✅ Yes | Pure deserialization, no DOM |
| `editorState.read(() => {...})` | ✅ Yes | Node traversal APIs work |
| `$getRoot()`, `$nodesOfType()` | ✅ Yes | Pure tree traversal |
| `node.decorate(editor, config)` | ✅ Yes (but useless) | Returns value, but headless disables decorator rendering |
| `editor.registerRootListener()` | ❌ No | Disabled in headless mode |
| `editor.registerMutationListener()` | ❌ No | Disabled in headless mode |
| `editor.registerDecoratorListener()` | ❌ No | Disabled in headless mode |
| `editor.focus()`, `editor.blur()` | ❌ No | Disabled in headless mode |
| `editor.getRootElement()` | ❌ No | Throws error in headless |

### 7. **Feasibility: Node Tree Walking in Headless**

**Can we walk nodes and call `node.decorate()`?** ✅ **YES**

Proof of concept (pseudo-code):
```typescript
import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
import { allHeadlessNodes } from '@haklex/rich-headless'
import { $getRoot, $nodesOfType } from 'lexical'

const editor = createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allHeadlessNodes })

// Parse JSON state
const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(jsonState)

// Walk nodes and call decorate()
editorState.read(() => {
  const root = $getRoot()
  const allNodes = root.getAllTextNodes()  // or $nodesOfType() for specific type
  
  for (const node of allNodes) {
    if ($isDecoratorNode(node)) {
      const decorated = node.decorate(editor, {})  // Returns null in headless
      // Process decorated value (will be null for all custom nodes)
    }
  }
})
```

**Why call `decorate()` in headless?**
- Could extract metadata/properties without React rendering
- Could validate node structure
- Could generate alternative formats (the project's existing use case with transformers)

### 8. **Existing Project Patterns**

The project **already uses headless-compatible patterns**:

**In `@haklex/rich-headless/src/index.ts`**:
- `stubDOM()` helper (line 50–52) returns dummy DOM elements for nodes that won't be displayed
- All DecoratorNodes return `null` from `decorate()` (lines 187–189, etc.)
- No React imports in decorator implementations

**In `@haklex/rich-headless/src/transformers.ts`**:
- `$toMarkdown()` function (lines 399–401) converts Lexical state to Markdown using transformers
- Already processes nodes without DOM:
  ```typescript
  export const IMAGE_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER: ElementTransformer = {
    export: (node) => {
      const j = node.exportJSON() as any
      return `![${j.altText}](${j.src})`  // Pure transformation
    }
  }
  ```

### 9. **Installation Requirement**

To use `@lexical/headless`:
```bash
npm install @lexical/headless
# or
pnpm add @lexical/headless
```

Then update `packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts` header (lines 7–10) to use the real function:
```typescript
import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'  // Currently just a comment
```

### 10. **Summary: Feasibility Assessment**

**✅ FEASIBLE: All core requirements can be met without browser APIs**

1. **Create headless editor**: `createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allHeadlessNodes })` ✅
2. **Parse JSON to nodes**: `editor.parseEditorState(json)` ✅
3. **Walk node tree**: `editorState.read(() => { $getRoot(), $nodesOfType(), etc. })` ✅
4. **Call `node.decorate()`**: Works but returns null for display-only nodes ✅
5. **No browser APIs required**: Pure Node.js server environment compatible ✅

**Current Usage in Project**: Already using markdown transformation via `$toMarkdown()` which works in headless mode. The framework is ready; only needs `@lexical/headless` package installation.

---

Sources:
- [@lexical/headless | Lexical](https://lexical.dev/docs/packages/lexical-headless)
- [@lexical/headless - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@lexical/headless)
agentId: ad85c446a8bff393e (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
<usage>total_tokens: 78965
tool_uses: 37
duration_ms: 149623</usage>
```
</details></tool-use>

Research how to use `@lexical/headless` in the Shiroi project:

1. Find and read the `@lexical/headless` package - look in node_modules for its types/API. Key function: `createHeadlessEditor`
2. Read `/packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts` again — it already uses allHeadlessNodes
3. Check if `createHeadlessEditor` is already used anywhere in the project
4. Find the Lexical types for `SerializedEditorState`, `LexicalEditor`, `EditorConfig`, `DecoratorNode`
5. Understand how `editor.parseEditorState(json)` works to deserialize JSON into node instances
6. Understand how `editorState.read(() => { ... })` works to traverse nodes
7. Check if `$getRoot()`, `$nodesOfType()`, and node iteration APIs work in headless mode (no browser)

The goal is to understand whether we can:
- Create a headless editor with `createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allNodes })`
- Parse editor state from JSON
- Walk the node tree and call `node.decorate(editor, config)` on decorator nodes
- All without browser APIs

Read the relevant Lexical source/types to confirm this is feasible.

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     7→  Spread,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { $insertNodes, DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    10→import { ImageIcon } from 'lucide-react'
    11→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    12→import { createElement } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { ImageRenderer } from '../components/renderers/ImageRenderer'
    15→import { createRendererDecoration } from '../components/RendererWrapper'
    16→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '../types/slash-menu'
    17→
    18→export type SerializedImageNode = Spread<
    19→  {
    20→    src: string
    21→    altText: string
    22→    width?: number
    23→    height?: number
    24→    caption?: string
    25→    thumbhash?: string
    26→    accent?: string
    27→  },
    28→  SerializedLexicalNode
    29→>
    30→
    31→function sanitizeImageSrc(src: string): string {
    32→  const trimmed = src.trim()
    33→  if (/^(javascript\s*:|vbscript\s*:|data\s*:(?!image\/))/i.test(trimmed)) {
    34→    return ''
    35→  }
    36→  return trimmed
    37→}
    38→
    39→function sanitizeColor(value: string | undefined): string | undefined {
    40→  if (!value) return undefined
    41→  const trimmed = value.trim()
    42→  if (/^#[\da-f]{3,8}$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    43→  if (/^(rgb|hsl)a?\([^)]+\)$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    44→  if (/^[a-z]{3,20}$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    45→  return undefined
    46→}
    47→
    48→export interface ImageNodePayload {
    49→  src: string
    50→  altText: string
    51→  width?: number
    52→  height?: number
    53→  caption?: string
    54→  thumbhash?: string
    55→  accent?: string
    56→}
    57→
    58→export class ImageNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    59→  __src: string
    60→  __altText: string
    61→  __width?: number
    62→  __height?: number
    63→  __caption?: string
    64→  __thumbhash?: string
    65→  __accent?: string
    66→
    67→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    68→    {
    69→      title: 'Image',
    70→      icon: createElement(ImageIcon, { size: 20 }),
    71→      description: 'Upload or embed an image',
    72→      keywords: ['image', 'picture', 'photo'],
    73→      section: 'MEDIA',
    74→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    75→        editor.update(() => {
    76→          $insertNodes([$createImageNode({ src: '', altText: '' })])
    77→        })
    78→      },
    79→    },
    80→  ]
    81→
    82→  static getType(): string {
    83→    return 'image'
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  static clone(node: ImageNode): ImageNode {
    87→    return new ImageNode(
    88→      {
    89→        src: node.__src,
    90→        altText: node.__altText,
    91→        width: node.__width,
    92→        height: node.__height,
    93→        caption: node.__caption,
    94→        thumbhash: node.__thumbhash,
    95→        accent: node.__accent,
    96→      },
    97→      node.__key,
    98→    )
    99→  }
   100→
   101→  constructor(payload: ImageNodePayload, key?: NodeKey) {
   102→    super(key)
   103→    this.__src = sanitizeImageSrc(payload.src)
   104→    this.__altText = payload.altText
   105→    this.__width = payload.width
   106→    this.__height = payload.height
   107→    this.__caption = payload.caption
   108→    this.__thumbhash = payload.thumbhash
   109→    this.__accent = sanitizeColor(payload.accent)
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   113→    const div = document.createElement('div')
   114→    div.className = 'rich-image-wrapper'
   115→    return div
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   119→    return false
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  isInline(): boolean {
   123→    return false
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedImageNode): ImageNode {
   127→    return $createImageNode({
   128→      src: serializedNode.src,
   129→      altText: serializedNode.altText,
   130→      width: serializedNode.width,
   131→      height: serializedNode.height,
   132→      caption: serializedNode.caption,
   133→      thumbhash: serializedNode.thumbhash,
   134→      accent: serializedNode.accent,
   135→    })
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  exportJSON(): SerializedImageNode {
   139→    return {
   140→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   141→      type: 'image',
   142→      src: this.__src,
   143→      altText: this.__altText,
   144→      width: this.__width,
   145→      height: this.__height,
   146→      caption: this.__caption,
   147→      thumbhash: this.__thumbhash,
   148→      accent: this.__accent,
   149→      version: 1,
   150→    }
   151→  }
   152→
   153→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   154→    return createRendererDecoration('Image', ImageRenderer, {
   155→      src: this.__src,
   156→      altText: this.__altText,
   157→      width: this.__width,
   158→      height: this.__height,
   159→      caption: this.__caption,
   160→      thumbhash: this.__thumbhash,
   161→      accent: this.__accent,
   162→    })
   163→  }
   164→}
   165→
   166→export function $createImageNode(payload: ImageNodePayload): ImageNode {
   167→  return new ImageNode(payload)
   168→}
   169→
   170→export function $isImageNode(
   171→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   172→): node is ImageNode {
   173→  return node instanceof ImageNode
   174→}
   175→

 `./packages/rich-headless/src/index.ts`

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * @haklex/rich-headless
     3→ *
     4→ * Headless-compatible Lexical node registry for server-side parsing.
     5→ * Zero React dependency — only lexical + standard @lexical/* packages.
     6→ *
     7→ * Usage with @lexical/headless:
     8→ *   import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
     9→ *   import { allHeadlessNodes } from '@haklex/rich-headless'
    10→ *   const editor = createHeadlessEditor({ nodes: allHeadlessNodes })
    11→ */
    12→
    13→import { CodeHighlightNode, CodeNode } from '@lexical/code'
    14→import { HorizontalRuleNode } from '@lexical/extension'
    15→import { AutoLinkNode, LinkNode } from '@lexical/link'
    16→import { ListItemNode, ListNode } from '@lexical/list'
    17→import { HeadingNode, QuoteNode } from '@lexical/rich-text'
    18→import { TableCellNode, TableNode, TableRowNode } from '@lexical/table'
    19→import {
    20→  DecoratorNode,
    21→  type EditorConfig,
    22→  ElementNode,
    23→  type Klass,
    24→  type LexicalEditor,
    25→  type LexicalNode,
    26→  type NodeKey,
    27→  type SerializedElementNode,
    28→  type SerializedLexicalNode,
    29→} from 'lexical'
    30→
    31→// ── Builtin nodes ──
    32→
    33→export const builtinNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
    34→  HeadingNode,
    35→  QuoteNode,
    36→  ListNode,
    37→  ListItemNode,
    38→  LinkNode,
    39→  AutoLinkNode,
    40→  HorizontalRuleNode,
    41→  TableNode,
    42→  TableCellNode,
    43→  TableRowNode,
    44→  CodeNode,
    45→  CodeHighlightNode,
    46→]
    47→
    48→// ── Helpers ──
    49→
    50→function stubDOM(): HTMLElement {
    51→  return document.createElement('span')
    52→}
    53→
    54→function extractText(state: unknown): string {
    55→  if (!state || typeof state !== 'object') return ''
    56→  const root = (state as Record<string, unknown>).root as
    57→    | Record<string, unknown>
    58→    | undefined
    59→  if (!root) return ''
    60→  return walkChildren(root)
    61→}
    62→
    63→function walkChildren(node: Record<string, unknown>): string {
    64→  const children = node.children as Record<string, unknown>[] | undefined
    65→  if (!children) return (node.text as string) ?? ''
    66→  return children.map(walkChildren).join('\n')
    67→}
    68→
    69→// ── SpoilerNode (ElementNode, inline) ──
    70→
    71→export class SpoilerNode extends ElementNode {
    72→  static getType(): string {
    73→    return 'spoiler'
    74→  }
    75→  static clone(node: SpoilerNode): SpoilerNode {
    76→    return new SpoilerNode((node as any).__key)
    77→  }
    78→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
    79→    super(key)
    80→  }
    81→  static importJSON(_json: SerializedElementNode): SpoilerNode {
    82→    return new SpoilerNode()
    83→  }
    84→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode {
    85→    return { ...super.exportJSON(), type: 'spoiler', version: 1 }
    86→  }
    87→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
    88→    return stubDOM()
    89→  }
    90→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    91→    return false
    92→  }
    93→  isInline(): boolean {
    94→    return true
    95→  }
    96→}
    97→
    98→// ── DetailsNode (ElementNode, block) ──
    99→
   100→export class DetailsNode extends ElementNode {
   101→  __summary = ''
   102→  __open = false
   103→
   104→  static getType(): string {
   105→    return 'details'
   106→  }
   107→  static clone(node: DetailsNode): DetailsNode {
   108→    const n = new DetailsNode((node as any).__key)
   109→    n.__summary = node.__summary
   110→    n.__open = node.__open
   111→    return n
   112→  }
   113→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   114→    super(key)
   115→  }
   116→  static importJSON(
   117→    json: SerializedElementNode & { summary?: string; open?: boolean },
   118→  ): DetailsNode {
   119→    const node = new DetailsNode()
   120→    node.__summary = json.summary ?? ''
   121→    node.__open = json.open ?? false
   122→    return node
   123→  }
   124→  exportJSON(): SerializedElementNode & { summary: string; open: boolean } {
   125→    return {
   126→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   127→      type: 'details',
   128→      summary: this.__summary,
   129→      open: this.__open,
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   133→    return stubDOM()
   134→  }
   135→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   136→    return false
   137→  }
   138→}
   139→
   140→// ── Generic headless DecoratorNode ──
   141→
   142→type Props = Record<string, unknown>
   143→
   144→function headlessDecorator(
   145→  type: string,
   146→  propKeys: string[],
   147→  defaults: Props,
   148→  inline = false,
   149→) {
   150→  class Node extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   151→    constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   152→      super(key)
   153→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   154→        ;(this as any)[`__${k}`] = defaults[k]
   155→      }
   156→    }
   157→    static getType(): string {
   158→      return type
   159→    }
   160→    static clone(node: Node): Node {
   161→      const n = new Node((node as any).__key)
   162→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   163→        ;(n as any)[`__${k}`] = (node as any)[`__${k}`]
   164→      }
   165→      return n
   166→    }
   167→    static importJSON(json: SerializedLexicalNode & Props): Node {
   168→      const node = new Node()
   169→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   170→        ;(node as any)[`__${k}`] = json[k] ?? defaults[k]
   171→      }
   172→      return node
   173→    }
   174→    exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode & Props {
   175→      const out: Props = { type, version: 1 }
   176→      for (const k of propKeys) {
   177→        out[k] = (this as any)[`__${k}`]
   178→      }
   179→      return out as SerializedLexicalNode & Props
   180→    }
   181→    createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   182→      return stubDOM()
   183→    }
   184→    updateDOM(): boolean {
   185→      return false
   186→    }
   187→    decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): null {
   188→      return null
   189→    }
   190→    isInline(): boolean {
   191→      return inline
   192→    }
   193→  }
   194→  return Node as unknown as Klass<LexicalNode>
   195→}
   196→
   197→// ── Simple property-based nodes ──
   198→
   199→export const ImageNode = headlessDecorator(
   200→  'image',
   201→  ['src', 'altText', 'width', 'height', 'caption', 'thumbhash', 'accent'],
   202→  {
   203→    src: '',
   204→    altText: '',
   205→    width: undefined,
   206→    height: undefined,
   207→    caption: undefined,
   208→    thumbhash: undefined,
   209→    accent: undefined,
   210→  },
   211→)
   212→
   213→export const VideoNode = headlessDecorator(
   214→  'video',
   215→  ['src', 'poster', 'width', 'height'],
   216→  { src: '', poster: undefined, width: undefined, height: undefined },
   217→)
   218→
   219→export const LinkCardNode = headlessDecorator(
   220→  'link-card',
   221→  ['url', 'source', 'id', 'title', 'description', 'favicon', 'image'],
   222→  {
   223→    url: '',
   224→    source: undefined,
   225→    id: undefined,
   226→    title: undefined,
   227→    description: undefined,
   228→    favicon: undefined,
   229→    image: undefined,
   230→  },
   231→)
   232→
   233→export const KaTeXInlineNode = headlessDecorator(
   234→  'katex-inline',
   235→  ['equation'],
   236→  { equation: '' },
   237→  true,
   238→)
   239→
   240→export const KaTeXBlockNode = headlessDecorator('katex-block', ['equation'], {
   241→  equation: '',
   242→})
   243→
   244→export const MermaidNode = headlessDecorator('mermaid', ['diagram'], {
   245→  diagram: '',
   246→})
   247→
   248→export const MentionNode = headlessDecorator(
   249→  'mention',
   250→  ['platform', 'handle', 'displayName'],
   251→  { platform: '', handle: '', displayName: undefined },
   252→  true,
   253→)
   254→
   255→export const CodeBlockNode = headlessDecorator(
   256→  'code-block',
   257→  ['code', 'language'],
   258→  { code: '', language: '' },
   259→)
   260→
   261→export const FootnoteNode = headlessDecorator(
   262→  'footnote',
   263→  ['identifier'],
   264→  { identifier: '' },
   265→  true,
   266→)
   267→
   268→export const FootnoteSectionNode = headlessDecorator(
   269→  'footnote-section',
   270→  ['definitions'],
   271→  { definitions: {} },
   272→)
   273→
   274→export const EmbedNode = headlessDecorator('embed', ['url', 'source'], {
   275→  url: '',
   276→  source: null,
   277→})
   278→
   279→export const CodeSnippetNode = headlessDecorator('code-snippet', ['files'], {
   280→  files: [],
   281→})
   282→
   283→export const GalleryNode = headlessDecorator('gallery', ['images', 'layout'], {
   284→  images: [],
   285→  layout: 'grid',
   286→})
   287→
   288→export const TldrawNode = headlessDecorator('tldraw', ['snapshot'], {
   289→  snapshot: '',
   290→})
   291→
   292→// ── Nested content nodes ──
   293→
   294→export class BannerNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   295→  __bannerType = 'note'
   296→  __contentState: unknown = null
   297→
   298→  static getType(): string {
   299→    return 'banner'
   300→  }
   301→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   302→    super(key)
   303→  }
   304→  static clone(node: BannerNode): BannerNode {
   305→    const n = new BannerNode((node as any).__key)
   306→    n.__bannerType = node.__bannerType
   307→    n.__contentState = node.__contentState
   308→    return n
   309→  }
   310→  static importJSON(
   311→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   312→      bannerType?: string
   313→      content?: unknown
   314→    },
   315→  ): BannerNode {
   316→    const node = new BannerNode()
   317→    node.__bannerType = json.bannerType ?? 'note'
   318→    node.__contentState = json.content ?? null
   319→    return node
   320→  }
   321→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   322→    return {
   323→      type: 'banner',
   324→      version: 1,
   325→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   326→      content: this.__contentState,
   327→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   328→  }
   329→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   330→    return stubDOM()
   331→  }
   332→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   333→    return false
   334→  }
   335→  decorate(): null {
   336→    return null
   337→  }
   338→  getTextContent(): string {
   339→    return extractText(this.__contentState)
   340→  }
   341→}
   342→
   343→export class AlertQuoteNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   344→  __alertType = 'note'
   345→  __contentState: unknown = null
   346→
   347→  static getType(): string {
   348→    return 'alert-quote'
   349→  }
   350→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   351→    super(key)
   352→  }
   353→  static clone(node: AlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
   354→    const n = new AlertQuoteNode((node as any).__key)
   355→    n.__alertType = node.__alertType
   356→    n.__contentState = node.__contentState
   357→    return n
   358→  }
   359→  static importJSON(
   360→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   361→      alertType?: string
   362→      content?: unknown
   363→    },
   364→  ): AlertQuoteNode {
   365→    const node = new AlertQuoteNode()
   366→    node.__alertType = json.alertType ?? 'note'
   367→    node.__contentState = json.content ?? null
   368→    return node
   369→  }
   370→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   371→    return {
   372→      type: 'alert-quote',
   373→      version: 1,
   374→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   375→      content: this.__contentState,
   376→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   377→  }
   378→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   379→    return stubDOM()
   380→  }
   381→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   382→    return false
   383→  }
   384→  decorate(): null {
   385→    return null
   386→  }
   387→  getTextContent(): string {
   388→    return extractText(this.__contentState)
   389→  }
   390→}
   391→
   392→export class GridContainerNode extends DecoratorNode<null> {
   393→  __cols = 2
   394→  __gap = '16px'
   395→  __cells: unknown[] = []
   396→
   397→  static getType(): string {
   398→    return 'grid-container'
   399→  }
   400→  constructor(key?: NodeKey) {
   401→    super(key)
   402→  }
   403→  static clone(node: GridContainerNode): GridContainerNode {
   404→    const n = new GridContainerNode((node as any).__key)
   405→    n.__cols = node.__cols
   406→    n.__gap = node.__gap
   407→    n.__cells = node.__cells
   408→    return n
   409→  }
   410→  static importJSON(
   411→    json: SerializedLexicalNode & {
   412→      cols?: number
   413→      gap?: string | number
   414→      cells?: unknown[]
   415→    },
   416→  ): GridContainerNode {
   417→    const node = new GridContainerNode()
   418→    node.__cols = json.cols ?? 2
   419→    const rawGap = json.gap
   420→    node.__gap = typeof rawGap === 'number' ? `${rawGap}px` : (rawGap ?? '16px')
   421→    node.__cells = json.cells ?? []
   422→    return node
   423→  }
   424→  exportJSON(): SerializedLexicalNode {
   425→    return {
   426→      type: 'grid-container',
   427→      version: 1,
   428→      cols: this.__cols,
   429→      gap: this.__gap,
   430→      cells: this.__cells,
   431→    } as SerializedLexicalNode
   432→  }
   433→  createDOM(): HTMLElement {
   434→    return stubDOM()
   435→  }
   436→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   437→    return false
   438→  }
   439→  decorate(): null {
   440→    return null
   441→  }
   442→  getTextContent(): string {
   443→    return this.__cells.map((cell) => extractText(cell)).join('\n')
   444→  }
   445→}
   446→
   447→// ── Aggregate ──
   448→
   449→export const customHeadlessNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
   450→  SpoilerNode,
   451→  DetailsNode,
   452→  ImageNode,
   453→  VideoNode,
   454→  LinkCardNode,
   455→  KaTeXInlineNode,
   456→  KaTeXBlockNode,
   457→  MermaidNode,
   458→  MentionNode,
   459→  CodeBlockNode,
   460→  FootnoteNode,
   461→  FootnoteSectionNode,
   462→  EmbedNode,
   463→  CodeSnippetNode,
   464→  GalleryNode,
   465→  TldrawNode,
   466→  BannerNode,
   467→  AlertQuoteNode,
   468→  GridContainerNode,
   469→]
   470→
   471→export const allHeadlessNodes: Klass<LexicalNode>[] = [
   472→  ...builtinNodes,
   473→  ...customHeadlessNodes,
   474→]
   475→
   476→// ── Transformers (Lexical → Markdown) ──
   477→
   478→export { $toMarkdown,allHeadlessTransformers } from './transformers'
   479→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type { ComponentType, ReactElement } from 'react'
     2→import { createElement } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import { useRendererConfig } from '../context/RendererConfigContext'
     5→import type { RendererConfig } from '../types/renderer-config'
     6→
     7→export type RendererKey = keyof RendererConfig
     8→
     9→type RendererPropsByKey = {
    10→  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]> extends ComponentType<
    11→    infer P extends object
    12→  >
    13→    ? P
    14→    : never
    15→}
    16→
    17→type RendererComponentByKey = {
    18→  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<
    19→    RendererConfig[K]
    20→  > extends ComponentType<any>
    21→    ? NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]>
    22→    : never
    23→}
    24→
    25→export type RendererWrapperProps = {
    26→  [K in RendererKey]-?: {
    27→    /** Name of the renderer in RendererConfig */
    28→    rendererKey: K
    29→    /** Default renderer component */
    30→    defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K]
    31→    /** Props to pass to the renderer */
    32→    props: RendererPropsByKey[K]
    33→  }
    34→}[RendererKey]
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Wrapper component that allows overriding default renderers with custom ones.
    38→ * Uses RendererConfig from context to determine which renderer to use.
    39→ */
    40→export function RendererWrapper({
    41→  rendererKey,
    42→  defaultRenderer: DefaultRenderer,
    43→  props,
    44→}: RendererWrapperProps) {
    45→  const config = useRendererConfig()
    46→  const Renderer: ComponentType<any> = config?.[rendererKey] ?? DefaultRenderer
    47→
    48→  return <Renderer {...props} />
    49→}
    50→
    51→/**
    52→ * Type-safe helper for creating RendererWrapper elements from .ts node files.
    53→ * Avoids the createElement + discriminated-union typing limitation.
    54→ */
    55→export function createRendererDecoration<K extends RendererKey>(
    56→  rendererKey: K,
    57→  defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K],
    58→  props: RendererPropsByKey[K],
    59→): ReactElement {
    60→  return createElement(RendererWrapper, {
    61→    rendererKey,
    62→    defaultRenderer,
    63→    props,
    64→  } as RendererWrapperProps)
    65→}
    66→
```

 `./packages/rich-ext-gallery/src/GalleryRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import 'react-photo-view/dist/react-photo-view.css'
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  decodeThumbHash,
     5→  type GalleryImage,
     6→  type GalleryRendererProps,
     7→} from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     8→import { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react'
     9→import type { ComponentType, UIEventHandler } from 'react'
    10→import { memo, useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react'
    11→import { useInView } from 'react-intersection-observer'
    12→import { PhotoProvider, PhotoView } from 'react-photo-view'
    13→
    14→import * as css from './styles.css'
    15→
    16→const IMAGE_CONTAINER_MARGIN_INSET = 60
    17→const CHILD_GAP = 15
    18→const AUTOPLAY_DURATION = 5000
    19→
    20→function throttle<T extends (...args: any[]) => void>(
    21→  func: T,
    22→  wait: number,
    23→): T {
    24→  let timeout: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null
    25→  return ((...args: Parameters<T>) => {
    26→    if (!timeout) {
    27→      timeout = setTimeout(() => {
    28→        func(...args)
    29→        timeout = null
    30→      }, wait)
    31→    }
    32→  }) as T
    33→}
    34→
    35→function clsx(...classes: (string | false | undefined | null)[]): string {
    36→  return classes.filter(Boolean).join(' ')
    37→}
    38→
    39→function useThumbhashStyle(
    40→  image: GalleryImage,
    41→): React.CSSProperties | undefined {
    42→  return useMemo(() => {
    43→    if (!image.thumbhash) return
    44→    const url = decodeThumbHash(image.thumbhash)
    45→    if (!url) return
    46→    return { backgroundImage: `url(${url})`, backgroundSize: 'cover' }
    47→  }, [image.thumbhash])
    48→}
    49→
    50→export const GalleryRenderer: ComponentType<GalleryRendererProps> = ({
    51→  images,
    52→  layout,
    53→}) => {
    54→  const [containerRef, setContainerRef] = useState<HTMLDivElement | null>(null)
    55→  const [, setUpdated] = useState({})
    56→  const memoedChildContainerWidthRef = useRef(0)
    57→
    58→  useEffect(() => {
    59→    if (!containerRef) return
    60→
    61→    const ob = new ResizeObserver(() => {
    62→      setUpdated({})
    63→      calChild(containerRef)
    64→    })
    65→
    66→    function calChild(containerRef: HTMLDivElement) {
    67→      const $child = containerRef.children.item(0)
    68→      if ($child) {
    69→        memoedChildContainerWidthRef.current = $child.clientWidth
    70→      }
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    calChild(containerRef)
    74→    ob.observe(containerRef)
    75→    return () => {
    76→      ob.disconnect()
    77→    }
    78→  }, [containerRef])
    79→
    80→  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(0)
    81→
    82→  const handleOnScroll: UIEventHandler<HTMLDivElement> = useCallback(
    83→    throttle<UIEventHandler<HTMLDivElement>>((e) => {
    84→      const $ = e.target as HTMLDivElement
    85→
    86→      const index = Math.floor(
    87→        ($.scrollLeft + IMAGE_CONTAINER_MARGIN_INSET + 15) /
    88→          memoedChildContainerWidthRef.current,
    89→      )
    90→      setCurrentIndex(index)
    91→    }, 60),
    92→    [],
    93→  )
    94→
    95→  const handleScrollTo = useCallback(
    96→    (i: number) => {
    97→      if (!containerRef) return
    98→
    99→      containerRef.scrollTo({
   100→        left: memoedChildContainerWidthRef.current * i,
   101→        behavior: 'smooth',
   102→      })
   103→    },
   104→    [containerRef],
   105→  )
   106→
   107→  const autoplayTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null>(null)
   108→  const isForward = useRef(true)
   109→  const autoplayRef = useRef(true)
   110→
   111→  const handleCancelAutoplay = useCallback(() => {
   112→    if (!autoplayRef.current) return
   113→
   114→    autoplayRef.current = false
   115→    if (autoplayTimerRef.current) {
   116→      clearInterval(autoplayTimerRef.current)
   117→    }
   118→  }, [])
   119→
   120→  const { ref } = useInView({
   121→    initialInView: false,
   122→    triggerOnce: images.length < 2,
   123→    onChange(inView) {
   124→      if (images.length < 2 || !autoplayRef.current) return
   125→
   126→      if (inView) {
   127→        autoplayTimerRef.current = setInterval(() => {
   128→          setCurrentIndex((prev) => {
   129→            if (prev + 1 > images.length - 1 && isForward.current) {
   130→              isForward.current = false
   131→            }
   132→            if (prev - 1 < 0 && !isForward.current) {
   133→              isForward.current = true
   134→            }
   135→
   136→            const index = prev + (isForward.current ? 1 : -1)
   137→            handleScrollTo(index)
   138→            return index
   139→          })
   140→        }, AUTOPLAY_DURATION)
   141→      } else {
   142→        if (autoplayTimerRef.current) {
   143→          clearInterval(autoplayTimerRef.current)
   144→        }
   145→      }
   146→    },
   147→  })
   148→
   149→  useEffect(
   150→    () => () => {
   151→      if (autoplayTimerRef.current) {
   152→        clearInterval(autoplayTimerRef.current)
   153→      }
   154→    },
   155→    [],
   156→  )
   157→
   158→  if (images.length === 0) return null
   159→
   160→  const layoutClass =
   161→    layout === 'masonry'
   162→      ? css.galleryMasonry
   163→      : layout === 'carousel'
   164→        ? css.galleryCarousel
   165→        : css.galleryGrid
   166→
   167→  if (images.length === 1 || layout === 'grid' || layout === 'masonry') {
   168→    return (
   169→      <PhotoProvider>
   170→        <div className={clsx(css.gallery, layoutClass)}>
   171→          {images.map((image, index) => (
   172→            <GalleryFigure key={index} image={image} />
   173→          ))}
   174→        </div>
   175→      </PhotoProvider>
   176→    )
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  return (
   180→    <PhotoProvider>
   181→      <div
   182→        className={clsx(css.gallery, css.galleryCarousel)}
   183→        ref={ref}
   184→        onTouchMove={handleCancelAutoplay}
   185→        onWheel={handleCancelAutoplay}
   186→      >
   187→        <div
   188→          className={css.galleryContainer}
   189→          onTouchStart={handleCancelAutoplay}
   190→          onScroll={handleOnScroll}
   191→          ref={setContainerRef}
   192→        >
   193→          {images.map((image, index) => (
   194→            <GalleryFigure
   195→              key={index}
   196→              image={image}
   197→              style={{
   198→                width: `calc(100% - ${IMAGE_CONTAINER_MARGIN_INSET}px)`,
   199→                marginRight: `${CHILD_GAP}px`,
   200→              }}
   201→            />
   202→          ))}
   203→        </div>
   204→
   205→        {currentIndex > 0 && (
   206→          <button
   207→            className={clsx(css.galleryNav, css.galleryNavPrev)}
   208→            onClick={() => handleScrollTo(currentIndex - 1)}
   209→          >
   210→            <ChevronLeft size={18} />
   211→          </button>
   212→        )}
   213→        {currentIndex < images.length - 1 && (
   214→          <button
   215→            className={clsx(css.galleryNav, css.galleryNavNext)}
   216→            onClick={() => handleScrollTo(currentIndex + 1)}
   217→          >
   218→            <ChevronRight size={18} />
   219→          </button>
   220→        )}
   221→
   222→        <div className={css.galleryIndicators}>
   223→          {images.map((_, i) => (
   224→            <div
   225→              className={clsx(
   226→                css.galleryIndicator,
   227→                currentIndex === i && css.galleryIndicatorActive,
   228→              )}
   229→              key={i}
   230→              onClick={() => handleScrollTo(i)}
   231→            />
   232→          ))}
   233→        </div>
   234→      </div>
   235→    </PhotoProvider>
   236→  )
   237→}
   238→
   239→const GalleryFigure = memo(
   240→  ({ image, style }: { image: GalleryImage; style?: React.CSSProperties }) => {
   241→    const thumbStyle = useThumbhashStyle(image)
   242→
   243→    return (
   244→      <PhotoView src={image.src}>
   245→        <figure className={css.galleryItem} style={{ ...thumbStyle, ...style }}>
   246→          <img
   247→            src={image.src}
   248→            alt={image.alt || ''}
   249→            width={image.width}
   250→            height={image.height}
   251→            loading="lazy"
   252→            style={{ maxWidth: '100%', height: 'auto' }}
   253→          />
   254→        </figure>
   255→      </PhotoView>
   256→    )
   257→  },
   258→)
   259→
   260→export default memo(GalleryRenderer)
   261→
```
</details></tool-use>

    16→import { CSS } from '@dnd-kit/utilities'
    17→import type { GalleryImage, GalleryRendererProps } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
    18→import { presentDialog, SegmentedControl } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
    19→import { usePortalTheme, vars } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
    20→import {

    27→  X,

    29→import type { CSSProperties, FC } from 'react'
